## 🧑 User

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/dev-server

# Dev Server — Start & Monitor

Start the Next.js dev server and stream errors from its log file.

## Steps

### 1. Kill any existing server on port 3000

```bash
fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
```

### 2. Start the server using Bash run_in_background

Use the Bash tool with `run_in_background: true`. Save the background task ID — the output file path is needed for the Monitor in step 4.

```
Bash(
  command:           "fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1; npm run dev",
  run_in_background: true
)
```

The tool returns a task ID and output path like:
`/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/<id>.output`

Save this path — it is used in step 4.

### 3. Wait until the server responds

```bash
until curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000 | grep -qE "200|302|307"; do sleep 1; done && echo "Server ready"
```

### 4. Start the persistent Monitor

Filter the log for errors and warnings only — do not emit every line.

```
Monitor(
  command:     "tail -f <output-path-from-step-2> 2>&1 | grep -E --line-buffered -i 'error|warn|failed|unhandled|exception|TypeError|ReferenceError|SyntaxError|cannot'",
  description: "Next.js dev server errors and warnings",
  persistent:  true
)
```

## On error notification

When the Monitor fires:

1. Read the last 60 lines of the log for full context:
   ```bash
   tail -60 .next/dev/logs/next-development.log
   ```
2. Identify root cause (missing table → DrizzleAdapter; JWEInvalid → session strategy mismatch; 42P01 → wrong table name, etc.)
3. Fix the code, verify with `npm run typecheck && npm run lint`
4. Inform the user of what was fixed.

## Important rules

- **Never print .env values** — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- Do not use `&` in Bash tool calls — use `run_in_background: true` or the Node spawn approach above
- `nohup ... &` and `... &` both fail with exit code 144 in this environment — always use the Node spawn detach pattern

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Änderungen:
- **Batch-Größe**: 128 → 16 (weniger Requests auf einmal)
- **Pause zwischen Batches**: 2 Sekunden
- **Retry-Backoff**: startet bei 5s statt 1s, bis zu 8 Versuche, max 60s Wartezeit

Die Ingestion dauert damit länger, aber sollte das Rate-Limit nicht mehr treffen. Poll-Timeout (4 Minuten) sollte für normale PDFs noch reichen — bei sehr großen Dokumenten müssten wir den Timeout hochsetzen.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   Building "YannBookLM" — a NotebookLM clone as a portfolio/job application project. In this session: completed Issue #18 (3-panel notebook layout + dark mode, commit cafc393) and Issue #6 (Web URL ingestion with Readability + Jina AI fallback, unified add-source modal, SHA-256 dedup, source rename/delete, commit 73e3df8). Currently debugging the PDF ingestion pipeline getting stuck at the "Einlesen & Indizieren" phase with real-time batch progress.

   **Security constraints (must be preserved verbatim):**
   - **NEVER put real tokens or secrets in chat**
   - **NEVER run commands that print .env values** — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
   - **NEVER edit .env files directly** — only point out mismatches to the user
   - Before every commit: Tests, ESLint, TypeCheck must be green — fix errors automatically
   - Each completed slice gets its own Git commit
   - Commit messages must be **single line only**, link the issue, **no Co-Authored-By**
   - **All AI prompts to Claude/Voyage AI must be in English**
   - **Use react-icons for icons** (e.g., `FaGithub`, `FaGoogle` from `"react-icons/fa"`), no inline SVG definitions
   - Chat history must be incrementally exported after each issue via `scripts/export-chat.py`

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Next.js 16** App Router, TypeScript strict, ESLint strict
   - **NextAuth v5 beta** with DrizzleAdapter, JWT strategy, Google + GitHub OAuth
   - **next-intl v4** for i18n, URL-based routing (`/de/...`, `/en/...`)
   - **@base-ui/react** (NOT Radix UI) — no `asChild` prop; use `buttonVariants()` className on trigger elements
   - **Tailwind CSS v4** with `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *))`, CSS variables for theming
   - **next-themes** with `defaultTheme="dark"`, `suppressHydrationWarning` on `<html>`
   - **Drizzle ORM** + PostgreSQL + pgvector (Supabase/Neon)
   - **pdfjs-dist v5.6.205** legacy ESM build — requires `workerSrc = file://${resolve("node_modules/pdfjs-dist/legacy/build/pdf.worker.mjs")}` in Node.js (empty string `""` no longer works in v5)
   - **@mozilla/readability + jsdom** for web scraping (Layer 1)
   - **Jina AI** `r.jina.ai/{url}` as bot-protection fallback (Layer 2)
   - **Voyage AI** `voyage-4-lite` embeddings via REST API — strict rate limits, needs retry + inter-batch delays
   - **AWS S3** presigned PUT URLs for PDF upload, CORS must be configured on bucket
   - **`after()` from next/server** for background ingestion after HTTP 202 response
   - **SHA-256 dedup** via `crypto.subtle.digest("SHA-256", buffer)` client-side before upload
   - **react-icons** for all icons (FaMicrophone, FaSlideshare, FaProjectDiagram, etc.)
   - Dev server: `Bash run_in_background: true` + Monitor for log watching

   - **`components/sources/add-source-modal.tsx`** (NEW) — Unified modal:
     ```tsx
     // Views: "pick" | "pdf-uploading" | "url-input" | "url-processing"
     // Width: sm:max-w-3xl p-6
     // Fancy title: gradient "Ihre Quellen in fundierte Antworten verwandeln" in DialogTitle
     // Pick view: web search bar (disabled), drag-drop zone, 4-button grid
     // PDF: computeSha256 → check dedup → presign → XHR upload → create source → pollStatus
     // URL: validate → check dedup → create source → pollStatus
     // pollStatus(sourceId, onProgress?): on 404 → throw immediately; updates embedProgress state
     // isProcessing = (view === "pdf-uploading" && progress < 100) || view === "url-processing"
     // Progress display: upload% → indeterminate → embedProgress%
     
     async function computeSha256(file: File): Promise<string> {
       const buffer = await file.arrayBuffer()
       const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.digest("SHA-256", buffer)
       return Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer)).map(b => b.toString(16).padStart(2, "0")).join("")
     }
     
     async function pollStatus(sourceId, onProgress?) {
       for (let i = 0; i < 120; i++) {
         await sleep(2000)
         const res = await fetch(`/api/sources/${sourceId}/status`)
         if (res.status === 404) throw new Error("Verarbeitung fehlgeschlagen")
         if (!res.ok) continue
         const { status, embedProgress } = await res.json()
         if (embedProgress > 0) onProgress?.(embedProgress)
         if (status === "ready") return
         if (status === "error") throw new Error("Verarbeitung fehlgeschlagen")
       }
       throw new Error("Timeout beim Verarbeiten")
     }
     ```

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`FiPresentation` doesn't exist in react-icons/fi**: Replaced with `FaSlideshare` from `react-icons/fa`

   - **`Label` component doesn't exist**: Replaced with `<label>` HTML element + className
   - **S3 CORS error (xhr.onerror)**: Told user to add CORS config to S3 bucket for `http://localhost:3000` with PUT + Content-Type

   - **Voyage AI 429 Too Many Requests**: Added retry with exponential backoff (5s * 2^attempt, max 60s, 8 retries), reduced batch size 128→16, added 2s delay between batches
   - **Poll timing out (4 min) on deleted source (returning 404)**: Added `if (res.status === 404) throw immediately`

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Auth split pattern** (from prior session): `auth.config.ts` (Edge/middleware) + `lib/auth.ts` (full with DrizzleAdapter)
   - **Background ingestion**: `after()` from `next/server` runs after HTTP response
   - **Bot-protected sites**: Two-layer scraping — Readability first, Jina AI fallback
   - **SHA-256 dedup**: `crypto.subtle` client-side hash computation before upload
   - **pdfjs in Node.js**: Must use legacy ESM build + explicit workerSrc path
   - **ONGOING**: "Einlesen & Indizieren" progress bar stuck — `embedProgress` never updates in modal. After `POST /api/sources 202`, no ingestion log entries appear in dev server logs, suggesting `after()` callback may not be executing or pdfjs is failing silently before first batch.

## 🤖 Assistant

Grün. Hier ist was sich geändert hat:

- **ChevronDown oben rechts**: Schließt das Modal — wenn der Server noch embeddet (Phase nach dem S3-Upload), wird es zu einem Mini-Item in der Sidebar minimiert, das Upload läuft weiter. Während des S3-Uploads selbst ist der Button disabled (XHR kann nicht übertragen werden).
- **X neben der Progress Bar**: Bricht den Upload ab — abortiert die laufende HTTP-Anfrage und löscht die Source (inkl. S3) aus der Datenbank.
- **Sidebar-Item**: Zeigt den Dateinamen + animierten Fortschrittsbalken. Die Sidebar pollt eigenständig alle 2s und lädt die Seite neu wenn `ready`.

## 🤖 Assistant

I see the root cause now. `positionStart` is nullable in the schema — and more critically, relying on `RETURNING` order from a large bulk insert is fragile. The cleanest fix is to generate parent chunk IDs client-side before inserting, eliminating the `RETURNING` lookup entirely:

---

## 🤖 Assistant

*[Tool: `Edit`]*
  - `replace_all`: False
  - `file_path`: /home/homior/yannickschroeder/yannbooklm_v2/lib/ingestion/pipeline.ts

## 🤖 Assistant

## 🧑 User

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/dev-server

# Dev Server — Start & Monitor

Start the Next.js dev server and stream errors from its log file.

## Steps

### 1. Kill any existing server on port 3000

```bash
fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
```

### 2. Start the server using Bash run_in_background

Use the Bash tool with `run_in_background: true`. Save the background task ID — the output file path is needed for the Monitor in step 4.

```
Bash(
  command:           "fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1; npm run dev",
  run_in_background: true
)
```

The tool returns a task ID and output path like:
`/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/<id>.output`

Save this path — it is used in step 4.

### 3. Wait until the server responds

```bash
until curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000 | grep -qE "200|302|307"; do sleep 1; done && echo "Server ready"
```

### 4. Start the persistent Monitor

Filter the log for errors and warnings only — do not emit every line.

```
Monitor(
  command:     "tail -f <output-path-from-step-2> 2>&1 | grep -E --line-buffered -i 'error|warn|failed|unhandled|exception|TypeError|ReferenceError|SyntaxError|cannot'",
  description: "Next.js dev server errors and warnings",
  persistent:  true
)
```

## On error notification

When the Monitor fires:

1. Read the last 60 lines of the log for full context:
   ```bash
   tail -60 .next/dev/logs/next-development.log
   ```
2. Identify root cause (missing table → DrizzleAdapter; JWEInvalid → session strategy mismatch; 42P01 → wrong table name, etc.)
3. Fix the code, verify with `npm run typecheck && npm run lint`
4. Inform the user of what was fixed.

## Important rules

- **Never print .env values** — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- Do not use `&` in Bash tool calls — use `run_in_background: true` or the Node spawn approach above
- `nohup ... &` and `... &` both fail with exit code 144 in this environment — always use the Node spawn detach pattern

---

## 🧑 User

Was genau ist der FK-bug?

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Das Problem: beim Einfügen von `child_chunks` in die Datenbank schlägt die Foreign-Key-Constraint fehl — `child_chunks.parent_chunk_id` zeigt auf eine ID die nicht in `parent_chunks` existiert.

Der Ingestion-Flow ist:
1. Parent Chunks in DB einfügen → IDs zurückbekommen
2. Child Chunks mit diesen IDs embedden + einfügen

Wir haben zwei Fixes versucht die nichts geholfen haben:
1. `RETURNING`-Reihenfolge war unzuverlässig → auf Map-Lookup per `positionStart` umgestellt
2. Map-Lookup auch unzuverlässig → UUIDs jetzt client-seitig generieren (kein `RETURNING` mehr nötig)

Trotzdem tritt der Fehler weiter auf. Deshalb haben wir Debug-Logging eingebaut das beim nächsten Upload folgendes ausgibt:
- Wie viele Parent Chunks eingefügt werden
- Alle Parent IDs nach dem Insert
- Welche Parent IDs in jedem Child-Batch referenziert werden

Damit können wir endlich sehen ob die IDs tatsächlich in der DB landen oder ob irgendwo eine UUID verloren geht. Wir brauchen dafür einfach einen Upload-Versuch mit dem Monitoring aktiv.

# Test-Driven Development

## Philosophy

**Core principle**: Tests should verify behavior through public interfaces, not implementation details. Code can change entirely; tests shouldn't.

**Good tests** are integration-style: they exercise real code paths through public APIs. They describe _what_ the system does, not _how_ it does it. A good test reads like a specification - "user can checkout with valid cart" tells you exactly what capability exists. These tests survive refactors because they don't care about internal structure.

**Bad tests** are coupled to implementation. They mock internal collaborators, test private methods, or verify through external means (like querying a database directly instead of using the interface). The warning sign: your test breaks when you refactor, but behavior hasn't changed. If you rename an internal function and tests fail, those tests were testing implementation, not behavior.

See [tests.md](tests.md) for examples and [mocking.md](mocking.md) for mocking guidelines.

## Anti-Pattern: Horizontal Slices

**DO NOT write all tests first, then all implementation.** This is "horizontal slicing" - treating RED as "write all tests" and GREEN as "write all code."

This produces **crap tests**:

- Tests written in bulk test _imagined_ behavior, not _actual_ behavior
- You end up testing the _shape_ of things (data structures, function signatures) rather than user-facing behavior
- Tests become insensitive to real changes - they pass when behavior breaks, fail when behavior is fine
- You outrun your headlights, committing to test structure before understanding the implementation

**Correct approach**: Vertical slices via tracer bullets. One test → one implementation → repeat. Each test responds to what you learned from the previous cycle. Because you just wrote the code, you know exactly what behavior matters and how to verify it.

```
WRONG (horizontal):
  RED:   test1, test2, test3, test4, test5
  GREEN: impl1, impl2, impl3, impl4, impl5

RIGHT (vertical):
  RED→GREEN: test1→impl1
  RED→GREEN: test2→impl2
  RED→GREEN: test3→impl3
  ...
```

## Workflow

### 1. Planning

When exploring the codebase, read `CONTEXT.md` (if it exists) so that test names and interface vocabulary match the project's domain language, and respect ADRs in the area you're touching.

Before writing any code:

- [ ] Confirm with user what interface changes are needed
- [ ] Confirm with user which behaviors to test (prioritize)
- [ ] Identify opportunities for deep modules (small interface, deep implementation) — run the `/codebase-design` skill for the vocabulary and the testability checks
- [ ] List the behaviors to test (not implementation steps)
- [ ] Get user approval on the plan

Ask: "What should the public interface look like? Which behaviors are most important to test?"

**You can't test everything.** Confirm with the user exactly which behaviors matter most. Focus testing effort on critical paths and complex logic, not every possible edge case.

### 2. Tracer Bullet

Write ONE test that confirms ONE thing about the system:

```
RED:   Write test for first behavior → test fails
GREEN: Write minimal code to pass → test passes
```

This is your tracer bullet - proves the path works end-to-end.

### 3. Incremental Loop

For each remaining behavior:

```
RED:   Write next test → fails
GREEN: Minimal code to pass → passes
```

Rules:

- One test at a time
- Only enough code to pass current test
- Don't anticipate future tests
- Keep tests focused on observable behavior

### 4. Refactor

After all tests pass, look for [refactor candidates](refactoring.md):

- [ ] Extract duplication
- [ ] Deepen modules (move complexity behind simple interfaces)
- [ ] Apply SOLID principles where natural
- [ ] Consider what new code reveals about existing code
- [ ] Run tests after each refactor step

**Never refactor while RED.** Get to GREEN first.

## Checklist Per Cycle

```
[ ] Test describes behavior, not implementation
[ ] Test uses public interface only
[ ] Test would survive internal refactor
[ ] Code is minimal for this test
[ ] No speculative features added
```

**Architektur:**

**MSW via `instrumentation.ts`** — Next.js 16 lädt diese Datei im Server-Prozess. Mit `msw/node` intercept ich dort alle ausgehenden `fetch`-Calls wenn `E2E_TEST=true` gesetzt ist:
- `POST https://api.voyageai.com/v1/embeddings` → deterministische 1024-dim Zufallsvektoren
- `https://r.jina.ai/*` → liefert den gecachten Korfu-HTML
- S3 `GetSignedUrl` → wird durch einen test-only Next.js Route Handler ersetzt (`/api/test/s3-upload`)
- S3 `DeleteObjectCommand` → 200 zurückgeben

**Test-DB:** `docker-compose.test.yml` mit `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` auf Port 5433. `global-setup` startet den Container, führt `db:push` aus und setzt ihn zwischen Testläufen zurück.

**Separate Playwright-Config** `playwright.test.config.ts`:
- Startet eigenen Next.js Server auf Port 3001 mit `E2E_TEST=true` + Test-DB-URL
- `reuseExistingServer: false`
- Timeout 10 Minuten (Ingestion kann dauern)
- Tests werden mit `@local` getaggt

**Fixtures:**
- `tests/fixtures/korfu.html` — der bereits gefetchte HTML-Inhalt
- `tests/fixtures/test.pdf` — ich erstelle ein kleines PDF mit `pdfkit`

**Tests in `tests/sources.spec.ts`:**
1. URL hinzufügen → auf `ready` warten → in Sidebar prüfen
2. PDF hochladen → auf `ready` warten → in Sidebar prüfen
3. Quelle umbenennen
4. Quelle löschen

**Code-Änderungen** an der App selbst (minimal):
- `lib/ai/voyage.ts`: `VOYAGE_API_URL` env var statt hardcoded URL (schon fast da)
- `lib/ingestion/web.ts`: `JINA_BASE_URL` env var statt `https://r.jina.ai`
- `app/api/sources/presign/route.ts`: in `E2E_TEST`-Mode URL zu lokalem Handler

## 🤖 Assistant

MSW für Server-Side bestätigt. Jetzt zum kniffligsten Teil: S3.

Der Ablauf beim PDF-Upload ist zweigeteilt:
1. **Server-side**: `POST /api/sources/presign` → AWS SDK generiert eine signierte S3-URL
2. **Browser-side**: XHR `PUT` direkt zur S3-URL

MSW/node in `instrumentation.ts` kann den AWS SDK-Call intercepten und eine beliebige URL zurückgeben. Aber das XHR vom Browser landet dann an dieser URL — und da greift `instrumentation.ts` nicht mehr.

Mein Vorschlag: `presign` gibt in `E2E_TEST`-Mode eine URL zurück, die auf einen Test-Route-Handler im selben Next.js Server zeigt (`/api/test/s3-upload`), der einfach 200 antwortet. Der Browser XHRt dann gegen localhost, kein echter S3-Call.

**Frage 2:** Akzeptierst du diesen Ansatz für S3 — MSW/node mockt den SDK-Call, der Browser-XHR geht gegen einen eigenen Test-Endpoint statt S3 — oder willst du lieber einen dedizierten Ansatz wie Localstack?

---

## 🧑 User

und der Test-Endpoint existiert dann nur in msw?

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Nein, das würde nicht funktionieren — MSW/node läuft im Next.js Server-Prozess, aber das Browser-XHR kommt von Playwright als echter HTTP-Request an. MSW/node interceptet nur `fetch`-Calls innerhalb von Node.js, nicht eingehende HTTP-Requests.

Der Test-Endpoint muss ein echter Next.js Route-Handler sein — aber nur aktiv wenn `E2E_TEST=true`:

```ts
// app/api/test/s3-upload/route.ts
export async function PUT() {
  if (process.env.E2E_TEST !== "true") return new Response(null, { status: 404 })
  return new Response(null, { status: 200 })
}
```

MSW/node mockt dann den AWS SDK-Presign-Call und gibt als URL `http://localhost:3001/api/test/s3-upload/[key]` zurück. Der Browser XHRt dahin, der echte Route Handler antwortet mit 200.

**Frage 3:** Ist das für dich akzeptabel — ein echter Route-Handler der in Produktion 404 zurückgibt — oder willst du lieber browser-seitiges MSW (Service Worker in Playwright) damit kein Test-Code in die App-Routen kommt?

---

## 🧑 User

Lieber kein Test-Code in den App-Routen

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Dann brauchen wir browser-seitiges MSW — einen Service Worker der in Playwright läuft und den XHR zur S3-URL interceptet.

Dafür gibt es zwei Wege:

**Option A — Playwright `page.route()`**: Playwright kann nativ jeden Browser-Request intercepten, ohne Service Worker. Kein App-Code nötig, funktioniert out-of-the-box. Aber es ist kein MSW.

**Option B — MSW Browser (Service Worker)**: `npx msw init public/` legt den Worker an. Der Worker muss aber im App-Code gestartet werden (z.B. in einem Client-Component conditional auf `NEXT_PUBLIC_MSW=true`). Das ist minimaler App-Code, aber in einer eigenen Datei — keine Test-Logik in Routes.

Meine Empfehlung: **Option B**, weil du gesagt hast die Abstraktionsschicht soll wiederverwendbar sein — z.B. für künftige Storybook- oder Komponenten-Tests.

**Frage 4:** Ist Option B für dich ok — MSW Browser-Worker gestartet in einer eigenen `lib/msw/browser.ts`, conditional auf `NEXT_PUBLIC_MSW=true`? Oder reicht dir Playwright `page.route()` wenn es nur für E2E-Tests ist?

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Gute Idee. Die Kombination wäre:

- **LocalStack** übernimmt S3 komplett — presign, Browser-XHR-Upload, delete. Der S3-SDK zeigt auf `http://localhost:4566`, presigned URLs zeigen ebenfalls dahin. Kein Browser-MSW nötig, kein Test-Endpoint in der App.
- **MSW/node** via `instrumentation.ts` mockt nur die reinen REST-APIs: Voyage AI + Jina AI.

App-seitiger Änderungsbedarf wäre minimal — `lib/s3/client.ts` bekommt einen `S3_ENDPOINT`-Env-Var-Override für LocalStack.

Meine Einschätzung: Das ist tatsächlich die sauberste Trennung. LocalStack verhält sich wie echtes S3, kein Fake-URL-Trick. MSW bleibt auf das beschränkt wofür es gut ist — REST-API-Mocking.

**Frage 5:** Akzeptierst du LocalStack + MSW/node als finales Modell — mit `docker-compose.test.yml` der beide Services (Postgres/pgvector + LocalStack) enthält?

## 🤖 Assistant

Das Projekt nutzt JWT-Strategie — `auth()` liest den Cookie direkt und verifiziert via Crypto, kein DB-Hit. Die `GET /api/auth/session` Route ist nur für client-seitige `useSession()`-Calls.

Das bedeutet: für Server-Komponenten und API-Routes kommt man nicht um den JWT-Cookie herum. MSW/browser kann `/api/auth/session` mocken — das hilft für Client-Side-Checks — aber den Cookie brauchen wir trotzdem.

Was du mit MSW zusätzlich gewinnen könntest: die NextAuth-Server-Calls zu Google/GitHub (`POST https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token`, `GET https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/userinfo`) via MSW/node intercepten — das würde den vollen Login-Flow testbar machen ohne echte OAuth-Credentials.

## 🤖 Assistant

Zurück zum Embedding-Thema. MSW/node mockt Voyage AI und kann deterministische 1024-dim Vektoren zurückgeben — kein lokales Modell nötig. Was wir damit verlieren: die Vektoren sind semantisch bedeutungslos, d.h. Similarity-Search-Tests wären nicht sinnvoll testbar.

Du hattest ursprünglich ein "lokales leichtgewichtiges Embedding-Modell" erwähnt. Das einzige Modell das in Node.js läuft und 1024 Dimensionen produziert wäre z.B. `Xenova/multilingual-e5-large` — aber das ist ~1.3GB und beim ersten Run langsam.

**Frage 8:** Reichen für jetzt deterministische Fake-Vektoren aus MSW (schnell, kein Download, testet nur ob Indexierung durchläuft) — oder willst du ein echtes lokales Modell weil du Similarity-Search in der Testuite haben willst?

## 🧑 User

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

**Continuing from previous session:**
- Fix "Einlesen & Indizieren" progress bar stuck (embedProgress never updating, `after()` callback possibly not executing)
- Test the URL ingestion feature
- Write Playwright E2E tests for source features

**In this session (completed):**
- Reduce inter-batch delay (2000ms→300ms) and retry backoff (5s→2s initial)
- Extend poll timeout to 300 attempts (10 min)
- Fix dedup check blocking re-upload of sources stuck in "processing"
- Show real ready-source count in ChatPanel
- Redesign add-source modal: ChevronDown to minimize to sidebar, X to cancel
- SourceSidebar tracks active upload with polling, shows progress, restores on page reload
- Fix FK violation in child_chunks (client-side UUID generation)
- Add YouTube icon for YouTube URL sources
- Build devTodo system: `POST /api/dev/todo` + `lib/dev-todo.ts` helper
- Add console.log /grilling reminders to all placeholder buttons

**Currently in progress:**
- Planning E2E test infrastructure via /grilling session
- Architecture decided: MSW/node (instrumentation.ts) + LocalStack (S3) + Docker PostgreSQL/pgvector
- Fixtures exported: kreta-embeddings.json (22 chunks), kreta-reisefuehrer.pdf, korfu.html

**Security constraints (MUST PRESERVE VERBATIM):**
- NEVER put real tokens or secrets in chat
- NEVER run commands that print .env values — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- NEVER edit .env files directly — only point out mismatches to the user
- All AI prompts to Claude/Voyage AI must be in English
- Before every commit: Tests, ESLint, TypeCheck must be green — fix errors automatically
- Each completed slice gets its own Git commit
- Commit messages must be single line only, link the issue, no Co-Authored-By

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- **Next.js 16** App Router, TypeScript strict, JWT auth strategy via NextAuth v5
- **MSW v2** via `instrumentation.ts` (msw/node) for server-side fetch interception (Voyage AI, Jina AI)
- **LocalStack** for S3 (real S3-compatible, browser XHR works without browser MSW)
- **Docker pgvector/pgvector:pg16** for test database on port 5433
- **AbortController** for clean XHR/poll cancellation
- **Client-side UUID generation** (`randomUUID()` from crypto) to avoid RETURNING ordering issues
- **devTodo pattern**: placeholder buttons → `devTodo(name)` → POST `/api/dev/todo` → `console.warn` picked up by monitor grep filter
- **Embedding fixtures**: pre-computed real Voyage AI embeddings from prod DB used as test fixtures
- **Playwright fixtures**: JWT cookie injection for pre-authenticated tests (no OAuth flow needed)
- **@local tag**: marks tests that can only run locally, not in CI

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`lib/ingestion/pipeline.ts`** (HEAVILY MODIFIED):
  ```ts
  import { randomUUID } from "crypto"
  const EMBED_BATCH_SIZE = 16
  const INTER_BATCH_DELAY_MS = 300  // was 2000

  async function ingestPages(sourceId: string, pages: PdfPage[]): Promise<void> {
    const parentChunkData = buildParentChunks(pages)
    // Client-side UUID generation - no RETURNING needed
    const parentsWithIds = parentChunkData.map((p) => ({
      id: randomUUID() as string,
      sourceId,
      content: p.content,
      pageNumber: p.pageNumber,
      positionStart: p.positionStart,
      positionEnd: p.positionEnd,
    }))
    await db.insert(parentChunks).values(parentsWithIds)
    const allChildren = parentChunkData.flatMap((parent, i) =>
      buildChildChunks(parent, i).map((child) => ({
        ...child,
        parentId: parentsWithIds[i].id,  // direct index, no Map lookup
      }))
    )
    // ... batch embedding loop with INTER_BATCH_DELAY_MS
  }
  ```

- **`lib/ai/voyage.ts`** (UPDATED retry backoff):
  ```ts
  const delay = retryAfter ? parseInt(retryAfter) * 1000 : Math.min(2000 * 2 ** attempt, 30_000)
  ```

- **`components/sources/add-source-modal.tsx`** (MAJOR REDESIGN):
  - `AbortController` ref for cancellation
  - `currentSourceIdRef`, `currentTitleRef`, `minimizingRef`, `sourceCreated` state
  - `handleCancel()`: aborts controller, catch block deletes source from DB+S3
  - `handleMinimize()`: sets `minimizingRef=true`, aborts, calls `onMinimize(sourceId, title)`, closes modal
  - `canMinimize = isEmbedding && sourceCreated` (state-based, not ref-based for render)
  - ChevronDown at top-right: minimize when embedding, close otherwise
  - X button next to progress bar in both pdf-uploading and url-processing views
  - `showCloseButton={false}` on DialogContent
  - `devTodo` calls on Drive, Kopierter Text, Web-Suche placeholders
  - `pollStatus` accepts `AbortSignal`, uses `DOMException("AbortError")` for clean abort detection

- **`components/notebook/source-sidebar.tsx`** (MAJOR REDESIGN):
  ```ts
  type ActiveUpload = { sourceId: string; title: string; embedPct: number }

  // Initialized from processing sources on page load:
  const processingSource = initialSources.find(s => s.status === "processing" || s.status === "pending")
  const [activeUpload, setActiveUpload] = useState<ActiveUpload | null>(
    processingSource ? { sourceId: processingSource.id, title: processingSource.title, embedPct: processingSource.embedProgress } : null
  )

  // Sidebar polling effect
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!activeUpload) return
    activeSourceIdRef.current = activeUpload.sourceId
    let cancelled = false
    async function poll() { /* polls /api/sources/[id]/status every 2s */ }
    poll()
    return () => { cancelled = true }
  }, [activeUpload?.sourceId])

  // Cancel from sidebar
  function handleCancelActiveUpload() {
    if (!activeUpload) return
    const { sourceId } = activeUpload
    setActiveUpload(null)
    deleteSource(sourceId, notebookId).catch(() => undefined)
  }
  ```
  - Processing/pending sources filtered OUT of main list (shown as activeUpload item)
  - YouTube icon: `FaYoutube` when url matches `youtube\.com|youtu\.be`
  - X button in sidebar active-upload item with progress bar

- **`components/notebook/chat-panel.tsx`** (UPDATED):
  ```tsx
  export function ChatPanel({ readySourceCount }: { readySourceCount: number }) {
    // Shows "1 Quelle" / "3 Quellen" with correct singular/plural
    // Different hint text when 0 vs >0 sources
  }
  ```

- **`app/[locale]/app/[notebookId]/page.tsx`** (NEW server component):
  ```tsx
  export default async function NotebookPage({ params }) {
    const { notebookId } = await params
    const session = await auth()
    if (!session?.user?.id) redirect("/login")
    const readySources = await db.select({ id: sources.id }).from(sources)
      .where(and(eq(sources.notebookId, notebookId), eq(sources.status, "ready")))
    return <ChatPanel readySourceCount={readySources.length} />
  }
  ```

- **`app/api/sources/check/route.ts`** (FIXED):
  ```ts
  // Only "ready" sources count as duplicates (not stale "processing" ones)
  .where(and(condition, eq(sources.status, "ready")))
  ```

- **`app/api/dev/todo/route.ts`** (NEW):
  ```ts
  export async function POST(req: Request) {
    if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "development") {
      return NextResponse.json({ error: "Not found" }, { status: 404 })
    }
    const { message } = (await req.json()) as { message: string }
    console.warn(`[WARN] Agent: User clicked '${message}' — starte jetzt eine /grilling session bevor du das implementierst.`)
    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true })
  }
  ```

- **`lib/dev-todo.ts`** (NEW):
  ```ts
  export function devTodo(feature: string) {
    if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "development") return
    fetch("/api/dev/todo", {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify({ message: feature }),
    }).catch(() => undefined)
  }
  ```

- **`components/notebook/notebook-header.tsx`**: devTodo on Freigeben, Einstellungen buttons
- **`components/notebook/studio-sidebar.tsx`**: devTodo on all STUDIO_TOOLS + Notiz hinzufügen
- **`tests/fixtures/kreta-embeddings.json`**: 22 child chunk embeddings (content + 1024-dim embedding) exported from prod DB
- **`tests/fixtures/kreta-reisefuehrer.pdf`**: PDF downloaded from S3 (386 bytes — may be HTML redirect, needs verification)

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **FK violation `child_chunks_parent_chunk_id_parent_chunks_id_fk`** (persisted across 3 fixes):
  - Fix 1: `RETURNING` order unreliable → switched to Map keyed by `positionStart`
  - Fix 2: `positionStart` nullable, potential collision → switched to client-side UUID generation with `randomUUID()`, eliminated RETURNING entirely
  - Added diagnostic logging, confirmed fix worked (19 batches, no error)
  - Removed logging after fix confirmed

- **`react-hooks/refs` lint error** (`canMinimize` accessed ref during render):
  - Added `sourceCreated` boolean state, set when sourceId is assigned
  - `canMinimize = isEmbedding && sourceCreated` instead of checking ref

- **Dedup blocking re-upload of failed sources**:
  - Source stuck in "processing" (catch block delete also failed) blocked re-upload
  - Fixed: dedup check only matches `status = "ready"`, not "processing"

- **Poll timeout (4 min) when source was deleted**:
  - Added `if (res.status === 404) throw immediately`
  - Extended poll to 300 attempts (10 min) for large PDFs

- **devTodo double message**:
  - `devTodo` was passing full message string, server was embedding it again
  - Fixed: `devTodo` only passes feature name, server formats the full message

- **kreta-reisefuehrer.pdf only 386 bytes**:
  - Presigned URL may have expired during download or returned an error page
  - Needs re-download with fresh presigned URL

5. Problem Solving:

- **FK violation root cause**: PostgreSQL's `RETURNING` does not guarantee row order matching insertion order. Client-side UUID generation (`randomUUID()` + direct array index) fully resolves this.
- **Minimize-to-sidebar**: React async functions continue after component state changes. `AbortController` + `minimizingRef` flag cleanly distinguishes cancel (delete) from minimize (hand off to sidebar).
- **Page reload progress persistence**: Sidebar initializes `activeUpload` from `initialSources` that have `status: "processing"`, immediately starts polling.

  - `playwright.test.config.ts`: separate config, port 3001, E2E_TEST=true, 10min timeout, @local tag
  - `tests/global-setup.ts`: start Docker services, run migrations, create test user
  - `tests/fixtures/korfu.html`: save fetched Korfu HTML
  - Re-download `tests/fixtures/kreta-reisefuehrer.pdf` (current file is only 386 bytes, likely error)
  - `tests/mocks/handlers.ts`: MSW handlers using kreta-embeddings.json fixture
  - `tests/sources.spec.ts`: PDF upload test + URL ingestion test (both @local tagged)
- **Issue #7**: YouTube ingestion (next after source tests)
- **Issue #8**: RAG Chat + Streaming + Citation Badges
- **Update memory**: remove "next session reminder" (URL test done, now in Playwright phase)

8. Current Work:

The /grilling session for E2E test architecture just concluded. The final architecture is:
- **MSW/node** via `instrumentation.ts`: intercepts Voyage AI + Jina AI server-side fetches
- **LocalStack**: real S3-compatible service in Docker (handles presign + browser XHR natively)
- **Docker PostgreSQL/pgvector**: test database on port 5433, reset between runs
- **Auth**: existing JWT cookie injection (pre-authenticated, no OAuth flow)
- **Embeddings**: pre-computed fixtures from prod DB (22 child chunks from kreta-reisefuehrer)
- **PDF fixture**: kreta-reisefuehrer.pdf (needs re-download, current file is 386 bytes)
- **URL fixture**: korfu.html (fetched, at /tmp/korfu.html)

Fixtures already exported:
- `tests/fixtures/kreta-embeddings.json` — 22 embeddings with content→vector mapping
- `tests/fixtures/kreta-reisefuehrer.pdf` — 386 bytes (likely broken, needs re-download)

## 🤖 Assistant

Alles committed. Um Tests zu starten:

```bash
npm run test:local
```

Das startet Docker (PostgreSQL + LocalStack), führt Migrationen aus, erstellt einen Test-User, startet Next.js auf Port 3001 mit `E2E_TEST=true` und MSW aktiv, und führt die vier Tests in [tests/sources.local.spec.ts](tests/sources.local.spec.ts) durch.

**Was wurde gebaut:**
- `docker-compose.test.yml` — pgvector auf Port 5433, LocalStack S3 auf Port 4566
- `instrumentation.ts` — MSW/node startet nur wenn `E2E_TEST=true` + `NEXT_RUNTIME=nodejs`
- `tests/mocks/handlers.ts` — Voyage AI (mit Fixture-Lookup, Error bei Miss), Korfu-HTML, Jina-Blocker
- `tests/fixtures/` — PDF (3.4MB), korfu.html, 22 Kreta-Embeddings, 17 Korfu-Embeddings
- `playwright.test.config.ts` — eigene Config, `*.local.spec.ts` Pattern, 10min Timeout
- `tests/sources.local.spec.ts` — 4 Tests: URL-Quelle, PDF-Quelle, Umbenennen, Löschen

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested: (1) Run the E2E test command and fix all errors until tests pass; (2) Build `npm run dev:test` script to start the test server on port 3001 with `E2E_TEST=true`; (3) Add pgAdmin to `docker-compose.test.yml`; (4) Upgrade to Node 24; (5) Throw errors in MSW when a fixture embedding is not found (instead of returning zero vector).

**Security constraints (MUST PRESERVE VERBATIM):**
- NEVER print `.env` values — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- NEVER edit `.env` files directly — only point out mismatches to the user
- All AI prompts to Claude/Voyage AI must be in English
- Before every commit: Tests, ESLint, TypeCheck must be green — fix errors automatically
- Each completed slice gets its own Git commit
- Commit messages must be single line only, link the issue, no Co-Authored-By

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Next.js 16** App Router, TypeScript strict, JWT auth strategy via NextAuth v5
   - **MSW v2** via `instrumentation.ts` (msw/node) — only activates with `E2E_TEST=true` + `NEXT_RUNTIME=nodejs`; `serverExternalPackages: ["msw"]` prevents client-side bundling
   - **LocalStack** (S3-compatible, port 4566) for presigned URLs + browser XHR
   - **Docker pgvector/pgvector:pg16** test DB on port 5433, pgAdmin on port 5434
   - **Production build for tests**: `next build && next start --port 3001` (not `next dev`) avoids Turbopack multi-instance lock conflict
   - **env-cmd** for loading `.env.test` non-secret vars; `AUTH_SECRET` inherited from parent process
   - **Fixed TEST_USER_ID** (`00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001`) to avoid JWT/DB mismatch across test runs
   - **Auto-opening modal**: `useState(initialSources.length === 0)` in SourceSidebar → modal opens automatically on empty notebooks; tests must close it before proceeding
   - **`networkidle` anti-pattern**: SourceSidebar polls every 2s → `waitForLoadState("networkidle")` never completes; use `domcontentloaded` instead
   - **`testIgnore`** in regular `playwright.config.ts` to prevent `*.local.spec.ts` from running against dev server (port 3000)
   - **Node 24.17.0** (LTS Krypton) — installed via nvm, `.nvmrc` and `engines` field updated
   - **Embedding fixtures**: pre-computed Voyage AI embeddings from prod DB stored as JSON; MSW throws `Error` on content miss

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`tests/fixtures/kreta-reisefuehrer.pdf`** (3.4MB)
     - Re-downloaded via fresh presigned URL (previous file was 386 bytes XML error response)
   
   - **`tests/fixtures/korfu.html`** (33KB)
     - Saved from `/tmp/korfu.html`

   - **`tests/mocks/handlers.ts`**
     ```ts
     import { http, HttpResponse } from "msw"
     import fs from "fs"
     import path from "path"
     import kretaEmbeddings from "../fixtures/kreta-embeddings.json"
     import korfuEmbeddings from "../fixtures/korfu-embeddings.json"

     const korfuHtml = fs.readFileSync(
       path.join(process.cwd(), "tests/fixtures/korfu.html"), // process.cwd() not __dirname (broken in prod bundle)
       "utf-8"
     )

     type RawFixture = { content: string; embedding: string | number[] }
     type EmbeddingFixture = { content: string; embedding: number[] }

     function parseFixtures(raw: RawFixture[]): EmbeddingFixture[] {
       return raw.map((r) => ({
         content: r.content,
         embedding: typeof r.embedding === "string" ? JSON.parse(r.embedding) : r.embedding,
       }))
     }

     const allFixtures: EmbeddingFixture[] = [
       ...parseFixtures(kretaEmbeddings as RawFixture[]),
       ...parseFixtures(korfuEmbeddings as RawFixture[]),
     ]

     function lookupEmbedding(content: string): number[] {
       const fixture = allFixtures.find((f) => f.content === content)
       if (!fixture) {
         throw new Error(
           `[MSW] No fixture embedding found for content (first 80 chars): "${content.slice(0, 80)}…"\n` +
             `Fixtures are out of sync — re-export embeddings from the database.`
         )
       }
       return fixture.embedding
     }

     export const handlers = [
       http.post("https://api.voyageai.com/v1/embeddings", async ({ request }) => {
         const body = (await request.json()) as { input: string[] }
         const embeddings = body.input.map((text) => lookupEmbedding(text))
         return HttpResponse.json({
           data: embeddings.map((embedding, index) => ({ embedding, index, object: "embedding" })),
           model: "voyage-4-lite",
           usage: { total_tokens: 0 },
         })
       }),
       http.get(
         "https://www.griechenland-urlaub.net/sehenswuerdigkeiten/insel_korfu.html",
         () => new HttpResponse(korfuHtml, { headers: { "Content-Type": "text/html; charset=utf-8" } })
       ),
       http.get("https://r.jina.ai/*", () =>
         HttpResponse.json({ error: "Jina AI should not be called in tests" }, { status: 500 })
       ),
     ]
     ```

   - **`instrumentation.ts`**
     ```ts
     export async function register() {
       if (process.env.E2E_TEST !== "true") return
       if (process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME !== "nodejs") return
       const { setupServer } = await import("msw/node")
       const { handlers } = await import("./tests/mocks/handlers")
       const server = setupServer(...handlers)
       server.listen({ onUnhandledRequest: "bypass" })
       console.log("[msw] Node server started for E2E_TEST mode")
     }
     ```

   - **`next.config.ts`**
     ```ts
     const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
       serverExternalPackages: ["msw"],
     }
     ```

   - **`lib/s3/client.ts`**
     ```ts
     export const s3 = new S3Client({
       region: process.env.AWS_REGION ?? "eu-central-1",
       ...(process.env.S3_ENDPOINT ? {
         endpoint: process.env.S3_ENDPOINT,
         forcePathStyle: true,
       } : {}),
       credentials: {
         accessKeyId: process.env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID!,
         secretAccessKey: process.env.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY!,
       },
     })
     ```

     services:
       postgres:
         image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
         environment:
           POSTGRES_USER: postgres
           POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
           POSTGRES_DB: yannbooklm_test
         ports:
           - "5433:5432"
         tmpfs:
           - /var/lib/postgresql/data
       localstack:
         image: localstack/localstack:3
         environment:
           SERVICES: s3
           AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: eu-central-1
           LOCALSTACK_AUTH_TOKEN: ""
         ports:
           - "4566:4566"
       pgadmin:
         image: dpage/pgadmin4
         environment:
           PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL: admin@test.local
           PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD: admin
         ports:
           - "5434:80"
         depends_on:
           - postgres

     export default defineConfig({
       testDir: "./tests",
       testMatch: "**/*.local.spec.ts",
       fullyParallel: false,
       retries: 0,
       workers: 1,
       globalSetup: "./tests/global-setup.test.ts",
       globalTeardown: "./tests/global-teardown.test.ts",
       use: { baseURL: "http://localhost:3001", trace: "on-first-retry" },
       timeout: 10 * 60 * 1000,
       expect: { timeout: 10 * 60 * 1000 },
       webServer: {
         command: "npm run dev:test",
         url: "http://localhost:3001",
         reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
         timeout: 5 * 60_000,

         stderr: "ignore",
       },
     })

   - **`playwright.config.ts`** (regular, for auth tests)

   - **`tests/sources.local.spec.ts`** — `createNotebook` helper (most critical fix):
     ```ts
     import path from "path"
     import type { Page } from "@playwright/test"
     import { test, expect } from "./fixtures.local"

     async function createNotebook(page: Page, name: string) {
       await page.goto("/de/app")
       await page.waitForLoadState("domcontentloaded")
       await page.getByRole("button", { name: "Neues Notizbuch" }).click()
       await page.getByPlaceholder("Name des Notizbuchs").fill(name)
       await page.getByRole("dialog").getByRole("button", { name: "Erstellen" }).click()
       const card = page.locator('[data-slot="card"]').filter({ hasText: name }).first()
       await expect(card).toBeVisible({ timeout: 30_000 })
       await card.getByRole("link", { name: "Notizbuch öffnen" }).click()
       await page.waitForURL(/\/de\/app\/.+/)
       // The add-source modal auto-opens on empty notebooks — close it so tests start clean
       const closeBtn = page.getByRole("dialog").getByRole("button", { name: "Schließen" })
       if (await closeBtn.isVisible({ timeout: 5_000 }).catch(() => false)) {
         await closeBtn.click()
       }
       return page.url().split("/").pop()!
     }
     ```

     DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/yannbooklm_test

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **msw/node bundled in browser**: Fixed with `NEXT_RUNTIME !== "nodejs"` guard in `instrumentation.ts` + `serverExternalPackages: ["msw"]` in `next.config.ts`
   - **TypeScript: embedding is `string` not `number[]`**: Fixed with `parseFixtures()` function using `JSON.parse()`
   - **Inline imports rejected by user**: Added top-level `import type { Page } from "@playwright/test"` instead
   - **pgvector extension missing**: Added `psql -c "CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector"` before `db:push` in global-setup
   - **`--no-turbopack` flag not valid in Next.js 16**: User suggested production build; changed to `npm run build && npm run start -- --port 3001`
   - **`__dirname` broken in production bundle**: Changed to `process.cwd() + "tests/fixtures/korfu.html"`
   - **JWT `JWTSessionError: no matching decryption secret`**: Added `AUTH_SECRET` to `.env.test` (inherited from parent process env)
   - **`UntrustedHost` error on localhost:3001**: Added `AUTH_TRUST_HOST=true` to `.env.test`
   - **`waitForLoadState("networkidle")` hangs forever**: SourceSidebar polls every 2s; changed to `domcontentloaded`
   - **"Quellen hinzufügen" button invisible**: `useState(initialSources.length === 0)` auto-opens AddSourceModal; fixed with close-button detection in `createNotebook`
   - **Tests ran against port 3000**: Regular `playwright.config.ts` had `testDir: "./tests"` without filter; fixed with `testIgnore: "**/*.local.spec.ts"`
   - **Duplicate email constraint**: Old user from previous run had different UUID but same email; fixed with `DELETE FROM users WHERE email = ... AND id != TEST_USER_ID` before INSERT
   - **User feedback "du musst nicht vor jedem befehl source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 24 && ausführen"**: Stop prepending nvm commands
   - **User feedback on dev:test script**: Use `./node_modules/.bin/env-cmd -f .env.test npm start -- --port 3001` pattern

5. Problem Solving:
   - The core problem was getting the E2E test infrastructure working end-to-end: production build → MSW intercepts Voyage AI → pre-computed fixtures return correct embeddings → source reaches "ready" → test passes
   - URL ingestion test (Test 1) now passes in ~4 seconds thanks to MSW mocking all external calls
   - PDF ingestion test (Test 2) timed out at 10 minutes — not yet debugged, was running when summary requested
   - Rename test (Test 3) failed at 1.1s — likely selector or UI interaction issue, not yet debugged

## 🤖 Assistant

## 🤖 Assistant

Committed. Der Root-Cause war **AWS SDK v3 v3.1073** das automatisch `x-amz-checksum-crc32` in presigned URLs einbaut — LocalStack 3 lehnt PUT-Requests ab wenn der Checksum-Wert nicht zur echten Payload passt. Fix: `requestChecksumCalculation: "WHEN_REQUIRED"` im S3 Client.

Alle 4 Tests laufen jetzt in ~30s durch.

## 🧑 User

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/dev-server

# Dev Server — Start & Monitor

Start the Next.js dev server and stream errors from its log file.

## Steps

### 1. Kill any existing server on port 3000

```bash
fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
```

### 2. Start the server using Bash run_in_background

Use the Bash tool with `run_in_background: true`. Save the background task ID — the output file path is needed for the Monitor in step 4.

```
Bash(
  command:           "fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1; npm run dev",
  run_in_background: true
)
```

The tool returns a task ID and output path like:
`/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/<id>.output`

Save this path — it is used in step 4.

### 3. Wait until the server responds

```bash
until curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000 | grep -qE "200|302|307"; do sleep 1; done && echo "Server ready"
```

### 4. Start the persistent Monitor

Filter the log for errors and warnings only — do not emit every line.

```
Monitor(
  command:     "tail -f <output-path-from-step-2> 2>&1 | grep -E --line-buffered -i 'error|warn|failed|unhandled|exception|TypeError|ReferenceError|SyntaxError|cannot'",
  description: "Next.js dev server errors and warnings",
  persistent:  true
)
```

## On error notification

When the Monitor fires:

1. Read the last 60 lines of the log for full context:
   ```bash
   tail -60 .next/dev/logs/next-development.log
   ```
2. Identify root cause (missing table → DrizzleAdapter; JWEInvalid → session strategy mismatch; 42P01 → wrong table name, etc.)
3. Fix the code, verify with `npm run typecheck && npm run lint`
4. Inform the user of what was fixed.

## Important rules

- **Never print .env values** — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- Do not use `&` in Bash tool calls — use `run_in_background: true` or the Node spawn approach above
- `nohup ... &` and `... &` both fail with exit code 144 in this environment — always use the Node spawn detach pattern

---

Alle drei Punkte erledigt:

**1. dev-server Skill** — `source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use` wird jetzt vor `npm run dev` ausgeführt (liest die `.nvmrc` → Node 24).

**2. YouTube-Icon Fix** — `SourceIcon` prüft jetzt `type === "youtube"` direkt, unabhängig von der URL. Das YouTube-Icon erscheint ab dem nächsten Page-Load.

**3. Transkript-Inhalt** — Das Video hat 4 Zeitfenster à 6 Minuten. Hier die Kapitel:

| Timestamp | Inhalt |
|-----------|--------|
| **00:00** | Einleitung + Rodos/Lindos, Lefkada (Vasiliki), Schildkrötenschutz |
| **06:00** | Santorin (Fira, Oia), Thessaloniki, Delphi, Kreta (Samaria-Schlucht) |
| **12:00** | Kreta (Strände), Athen (Akropolis, antikes Theater), Mykonos |
| **18:00** | Kefalonia (Fiskardo, Argostoli) + Outro |

## 🧑 User

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The session resumed from a previous context with the goal of fixing 3 failing E2E tests (PDF upload, rename, delete), then implementing Issue #7 (YouTube ingestion), and starting Issue #8 (RAG Chat). Specific requests:
   - Fix all E2E test errors until all 4 pass (user said "führe den test befehl selber aus, reagiere auf fehler bis er funktioniert")
   - User reported: "wir kriegen beim hochladen einer pdf s3 400. Ist localstack wohlmöglich falsch konfiguriert?" (PDF upload returns S3 400)
   - Implement Issue #7 YouTube ingestion end-to-end with E2E test
   - Fix YouTube icon showing as PDF icon in sidebar
   - Update dev-server skill with `nvm use`
   - Show transcript content from "Die 13 UNGLAUBLICHSTEN Orte Griechenlands" YouTube video
   - Persist chat/memory at session end
   - "Das soll am Ende eines jeden Features passieren" (export chat at end of every feature)
   - Move to Issue #8 (RAG Chat)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **AWS SDK v3 v3.1073**: Automatically adds `x-amz-checksum-crc32` + `x-amz-sdk-checksum-algorithm=CRC32` to presigned PUT URLs; fix: `requestChecksumCalculation: "WHEN_REQUIRED"` + `responseChecksumValidation: "WHEN_REQUIRED"`
   - **LocalStack 3**: Rejects PUT requests with invalid CRC32 checksum (400 InvalidRequest)
   - **youtube-transcript v1.3.1**: Uses InnerTube API (POST `https://www.youtube.com/youtubei/v1/player`), returns segments with `offset` (ms) and `duration` (ms)
   - **YouTube chunking**: 120-second time windows → pages with `pageNumber = startSeconds`, fed into existing `buildParentChunks`/`buildChildChunks`; `positionStart` on child chunks = parent's startSeconds (for timestamp citations in Issue #8)
   - **oEmbed**: `https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=...&format=json` for video title (no API key required)
   - **MSW v2**: srv3 XML format (`<p t="ms" d="ms">`) for YouTube transcript mocking; InnerTube response mocked with fake captionTracks pointing to `https://www.youtube.com/api/timedtext?test=yt-e2e`
   - **Playwright E2E**: 5/5 tests passing in 33s (URL add, PDF add, rename, YouTube add, delete)
   - **Next.js 16 App Router** with `after()` for background ingestion
   - **pgvector**: embeddings stored as 1024-dim vectors; `position_start` for YouTube = seconds not character offset
   - **Vercel AI SDK**: `ai@6.0.208`, `@ai-sdk/anthropic@3.0.85` installed (for Issue #8)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`lib/s3/client.ts`** — Fixed AWS SDK v3 CRC32 checksum issue:
     ```ts
     export const s3 = new S3Client({
       region: process.env.AWS_REGION ?? "eu-central-1",
       ...(process.env.S3_ENDPOINT ? {
         endpoint: process.env.S3_ENDPOINT,
         forcePathStyle: true,
       } : {}),
       credentials: {
         accessKeyId: process.env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID!,
         secretAccessKey: process.env.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY!,
       },
       // AWS SDK v3 adds CRC32 checksums by default; LocalStack rejects them
       requestChecksumCalculation: "WHEN_REQUIRED",
       responseChecksumValidation: "WHEN_REQUIRED",
     })
     export const S3_BUCKET = (process.env.AWS_S3_BUCKET ?? process.env.S3_BUCKET)!
     ```

   - **`tests/global-setup.test.ts`** — Added checksum fix + BucketAlreadyOwnedByYou handling + stale user deletion:
     ```ts
     await sql`DELETE FROM users WHERE email = ${TEST_USER_EMAIL} AND id != ${TEST_USER_ID}`
     // ...
     const s3 = new S3Client({
       region: "eu-central-1",
       endpoint: "http://localhost:4566",
       forcePathStyle: true,
       credentials: { accessKeyId: "test", secretAccessKey: "test" },
       requestChecksumCalculation: "WHEN_REQUIRED",
       responseChecksumValidation: "WHEN_REQUIRED",
     })
     try {
       await s3.send(new CreateBucketCommand({ Bucket: TEST_BUCKET }))
     } catch (e: unknown) {
       if ((e as { name?: string }).name !== "BucketAlreadyOwnedByYou") throw e
     }
     ```

   - **`lib/ingestion/youtube.ts`** — New file, YouTube transcript fetching and chunking:
     ```ts
     const WINDOW_SECONDS = 120
     export function extractVideoId(url: string): string | null {
       const m = url.match(/(?:youtube\.com\/watch\?.*v=|youtu\.be\/)([\w-]{11})/)
       return m ? m[1] : null
     }
     export async function fetchYoutubeContent(url: string): Promise<{
       title: string
       parentChunks: (ParentChunk & { startSeconds: number })[]
       childChunks: ChildChunk[]
     }> { ... }
     ```

   - **`lib/ingestion/pipeline.ts`** — Added `ingestYoutube`, `ingestPrebuiltChunks`, extracted `embedAndInsertChildren`:
     ```ts
     export async function ingestYoutube(sourceId: string, url: string): Promise<void> {
       try {
         await db.update(sources).set({ status: "processing" }).where(eq(sources.id, sourceId))
         const { title, parentChunks: parents, childChunks: children } = await fetchYoutubeContent(url)
         await db.update(sources).set({ title }).where(eq(sources.id, sourceId))
         await ingestPrebuiltChunks(sourceId, parents, children)
         await db.update(sources).set({ status: "ready" }).where(eq(sources.id, sourceId))
       } catch (err) {
         console.error(`[ingest] YouTube ingestion failed for source ${sourceId}:`, err)
         await db.delete(sources).where(eq(sources.id, sourceId))
       }
     }
     ```

   - **`app/api/sources/route.ts`** — Auto-detects YouTube URL, routes to `ingestYoutube`:
     ```ts
     const isYoutube = extractVideoId(body.url) !== null
     const sourceType = isYoutube ? "youtube" : "url"
     // ...stores with correct type, calls ingestYoutube or ingestUrl in after()
     ```

   - **`tests/mocks/handlers.ts`** — Added YouTube MSW mocks:
     ```ts
     const YOUTUBE_TEST_VIDEO_ID = "dQw4w9WgXcQ"
     const YOUTUBE_TEST_TITLE = "Kreta Reiseführer"
     const YOUTUBE_TRANSCRIPT_URL = "https://www.youtube.com/api/timedtext?test=yt-e2e"
     const YOUTUBE_TRANSCRIPT_XML = `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
     <timedtext><body>
     <p t="0" d="4000">Kreta ist die größte Insel Griechenlands.</p>
     ...
     </body></timedtext>`
     // Handlers: InnerTube POST, timedtext GET, oEmbed GET
     ```

   - **`tests/fixtures/youtube-test-embeddings.json`** — Synthetic embedding (unit vector) for 1 YouTube child chunk

   - **`tests/sources.local.spec.ts`** — Added YouTube test (5th test):
     ```ts
     const YOUTUBE_URL = "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ"
     test("YouTube-Quelle hinzufügen und indizieren", async ({ page }) => {
       await createNotebook(page, "E2E YouTube Test")
       await page.getByRole("button", { name: "Quellen hinzufügen" }).click()
       await page.getByRole("button", { name: "Websites" }).click()
       await page.getByPlaceholder("https://example.com/artikel").fill(YOUTUBE_URL)
       await page.getByRole("button", { name: "Hinzufügen" }).click()
       await expect(page.locator("aside").getByText("Kreta Reiseführer", { exact: false }))
         .toBeVisible({ timeout: 10 * 60 * 1000 })
       await deleteNotebook(page, "E2E YouTube Test")
     })
     ```

   - **`components/notebook/source-sidebar.tsx`** — Fixed YouTube icon (removed URL-based detection, use `type` directly):
     ```tsx
     function SourceIcon({ type, status }: { type: Source["type"]; status: Source["status"] }) {
       if (status === "processing" || status === "pending") {
         return <Loader2 className="size-4 shrink-0 animate-spin text-muted-foreground" />
       }
       if (type === "youtube") return <FaYoutube className="size-4 shrink-0 text-red-500" />
       if (type === "url") return <Globe className="size-4 shrink-0 text-blue-400" />
       return <FileText className="size-4 shrink-0 text-red-400" />
     }
     ```

   - **`/home/homior/.claude/skills/dev-server/SKILL.md`** — Added `nvm use` to server start command:
     ```
     command: "fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1; source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use && npm run dev"
     ```

   - **`components/notebook/chat-panel.tsx`** — Existing placeholder (no real functionality yet); currently shows disabled input and "Der Chat wird in einem zukünftigen Issue implementiert."

   - **`app/[locale]/app/[notebookId]/page.tsx`** — Passes `readySourceCount` to ChatPanel; no `/api/chat` route exists yet

   - **`lib/ai/voyage.ts`** — Existing `embedText(text)` and `embedBatch(texts)` functions using Voyage AI API; will be used for query embedding in Issue #8

   - **`db/schema.ts`** — `messages` and `messageCitations` tables already defined:
     ```ts
     export const messages = pgTable("messages", {
       id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
       notebookId: uuid("notebook_id").notNull().references(() => notebooks.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
       role: messageRoleEnum("role").notNull(),
       content: text("content").notNull(),
       createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { mode: "date" }).notNull().defaultNow(),
     })
     export const messageCitations = pgTable("message_citations", {
       id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
       messageId: uuid("message_id").notNull().references(() => messages.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
       childChunkId: uuid("child_chunk_id").notNull().references(() => childChunks.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
       orderIndex: integer("order_index").notNull(),
     })
     ```

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **S3 400 on PDF upload (primary bug)**: AWS SDK v3 v3.1073 auto-adds `x-amz-checksum-crc32` to presigned URLs. LocalStack returns 400 `InvalidRequest`. Fixed by adding `requestChecksumCalculation: "WHEN_REQUIRED"` + `responseChecksumValidation: "WHEN_REQUIRED"` to BOTH `lib/s3/client.ts` AND the S3Client in `tests/global-setup.test.ts`.
   - **BucketAlreadyOwnedByYou in global-setup**: When bucket exists from manual testing between runs. Fixed with try/catch checking `e.name !== "BucketAlreadyOwnedByYou"`.
   - **Tests 3+4 ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED**: Server was killed during Test 2's 10-minute timeout. Once S3 was fixed, Test 2 completed in ~4s, server stayed alive.
   - **YouTube icon shows as PDF**: `SourceIcon` checked `type === "url"` and URL pattern for YouTube, but sources now have `type === "youtube"`. Fixed by checking `type` directly.
   - **dev-server Node version warning**: Dev server running with Node 20 despite Node 24 being configured. User requested `nvm use` added to the dev-server skill. Added `source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use &&` prefix to the start command.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Traced S3 400 error by reproducing it manually with curl: presigned URL contained `x-amz-checksum-crc32=AAAAAA%3D%3D` (zero bytes CRC32) but payload had real content.
   - Verified kreta PDF chunk fixture integrity (22/22 ALL MATCH) before pursuing OOM theory — confirmed the real issue was the S3 400.
   - YouTube chunking design decision: used `pageNumber = startSeconds` (inherited by child chunks) and overrode `positionStart` to seconds on parent chunks; child chunks get parent's startSeconds as their `positionStart` (±2 min precision, acceptable for MVP timestamp links).
   - Issue #8 exploration complete: `chat-panel.tsx` is a placeholder, `/api/chat` doesn't exist, `messages` + `messageCitations` tables already in schema, Vercel AI SDK installed.

## 🤖 Assistant

Issue #8 ist implementiert und committed. Hier was gebaut wurde:

**`/api/chat` Route** ([app/api/chat/route.ts](app/api/chat/route.ts))
- Auth-Check + Notebook-Owner-Verifizierung
- Voyage AI Embedding der User-Frage
- pgvector Cosine-Similarity-Search via `cosineDistance()` aus drizzle-orm → Top-5 Parent-Chunk-Kontexte
- Claude `claude-sonnet-4-6` Streaming mit deutschen System-Prompt + Zitat-Instruktionen (`[1]`, `[2]`)
- `onFinish`: speichert User- und Assistant-Message in DB + parsed `[N]`-Marker → `message_citations`
- `messageMetadata` sendet CitationChunk-Metadaten zum Client (sourceTitle, type, url, positionStart, pageNumber)

**ChatPanel** ([components/notebook/chat-panel.tsx](components/notebook/chat-panel.tsx))
- `useChat<ChatMessage>` via `DefaultChatTransport` (AI SDK v6 API)
- Streaming mit Spinner während `submitted`/`streaming`-Status
- Inline-Zitat-Badges: Text wird auf `[N]`-Gruppen gesplittet → kreisförmige Badges mit expand/collapse bei >3
- Citation-Preview-Panel: klappt rechts auf bei Badge-Klick, zeigt Quelltext + YouTube-Timestamp-Link / PDF-Seite / URL-Domain
- Deaktiviertes Input wenn keine Quellen ready

## 🧑 User

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/dev-server

# Dev Server — Start & Monitor

Start the Next.js dev server and stream errors from its log file.

## Steps

### 1. Kill any existing server on port 3000

```bash
fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
```

### 2. Start the server using Bash run_in_background

Use the Bash tool with `run_in_background: true`. Save the background task ID — the output file path is needed for the Monitor in step 4.

Load the correct Node version via nvm before starting:

```
Bash(
  command:           "fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1; source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use && npm run dev",
  run_in_background: true
)
```

The tool returns a task ID and output path like:
`/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/<id>.output`

Save this path — it is used in step 4.

### 3. Wait until the server responds

```bash
until curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000 | grep -qE "200|302|307"; do sleep 1; done && echo "Server ready"
```

### 4. Start the persistent Monitor

Filter the log for errors and warnings only — do not emit every line.

```
Monitor(
  command:     "tail -f <output-path-from-step-2> 2>&1 | grep -E --line-buffered -i 'error|warn|failed|unhandled|exception|TypeError|ReferenceError|SyntaxError|cannot'",
  description: "Next.js dev server errors and warnings",
  persistent:  true
)
```

## On error notification

When the Monitor fires:

1. Read the last 60 lines of the log for full context:
   ```bash
   tail -60 .next/dev/logs/next-development.log
   ```
2. Identify root cause (missing table → DrizzleAdapter; JWEInvalid → session strategy mismatch; 42P01 → wrong table name, etc.)
3. Fix the code, verify with `npm run typecheck && npm run lint`
4. Inform the user of what was fixed.

## Important rules

- **Never print .env values** — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- Do not use `&` in Bash tool calls — use `run_in_background: true` or the Node spawn approach above
- `nohup ... &` and `... &` both fail with exit code 144 in this environment — always use the Node spawn detach pattern

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Fertig. Hier was implementiert wurde:

**Hover-Popover auf Citation-Badges** — beim Hovern (300ms Delay) erscheint eine Karte mit:
1. Quellenname + Icon
2. Chunk-Content als Markdown (scrollbar, max 224px)
3. „Quelle anzeigen"-Button (öffnet Sidebar-Detail)

**Sidebar-Detailansicht** — beim Klick auf ein Badge oder „Quelle anzeigen":
- Sidebar expandiert automatisch wenn sie kollabiert war
- Quellenliste wird durch das Detail-Panel ersetzt (3 Sektionen mit Trennstrichen)
- `ChevronLeft`-Button oben links kehrt zur Quellenliste zurück
- Content als Markdown gerendert (inkl. Bilder falls vorhanden)
- Footer: klickbarer „Quelle anzeigen"-Link (YouTube mit Timestamp, URL mit Domain, PDF mit Seitenzahl)

Die Kommunikation läuft über `window.dispatchEvent` ([lib/source-view-event.ts](lib/source-view-event.ts)) — kein shared state, keine URL-Params.

## 🧑 User

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The session continued Issue #8 (RAG Chat) implementation and addressed several follow-up bugs/features:
- Implement RAG Chat: `/api/chat` route with pgvector retrieval + Claude streaming + citation badges
- Render chat messages as Markdown (`react-markdown` with citation badges inline)
- Fix empty chat history on page load (load from DB as `initialMessages`)
- Add citation hover preview modal (3-section: name, content, "Quelle anzeigen" button)
- Add source detail view in left sidebar when badge is clicked (with minimize/back button)
- **Latest request (pending):** Widen both left and right sidebars; fix the expand/collapse trigger for citation groups that doesn't render when >3 citations are shown

**Security constraints (verbatim, must remain in effect):**
- NEVER print `.env` values — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- NEVER edit `.env` files directly — only point out mismatches to the user
- All AI prompts to Claude/Voyage AI must be in English
- Before every commit: Tests, ESLint, TypeCheck must be green — fix errors automatically
- Each completed slice gets its own Git commit
- Commit messages must be single line only, link the issue, no Co-Authored-By
- At end of every feature: `python3 scripts/export-chat.py --label "Issue #N <Name>"` and commit

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- **AI SDK v6** (`ai@6.0.208`, `@ai-sdk/react`): `useChat` from `@ai-sdk/react`, `DefaultChatTransport` for HTTP, `streamText(...).toUIMessageStreamResponse<UIMessage<ChatMeta>>()` for server streaming, `messageMetadata` option sends citation chunk data to client, `convertToModelMessages` converts UIMessage[] to ModelMessages
- **pgvector + Drizzle ORM**: `cosineDistance(column, embedding[])` from `drizzle-orm` for similarity search, returns `SQL` usable in `.orderBy(asc(...))`
- **react-markdown v10**: Default export (`import Markdown from "react-markdown"`), NOT named export. `rehype-raw` to allow inline HTML. Citation badges injected via `text.replace(/\[(\d+)\]/g, '<cite data-idx="$1"></cite>')` + custom `cite` component handler
- **base-ui `@base-ui/react`**: shadcn's `hover-card` uses `PreviewCard` from base-ui (not Radix). `HoverCardTrigger` has `delay`/`closeDelay` props (not on Root); no `asChild` — use `render={<span />}` instead
- **Custom window events**: `lib/source-view-event.ts` uses `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("yannbooklm:source-view", { detail: chunk }))` to communicate between ChatPanel (sender) and SourceSidebar (listener) — sibling components in the layout
- **ChatMessage / UIMessage<ChatMeta>**: `ChatMeta = { citations?: CitationChunk[] }`, citations stored as `messageMetadata` in stream, accessible as `msg.metadata?.citations` in `useChat`
- **DB message history loading**: Server component `page.tsx` loads messages with LEFT JOIN on messageCitations → childChunks → parentChunks → sources; groups rows by message id; converts to `ChatMessage[]` with `parts: [{ type: "text", text: content, state: "done" }]`

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`lib/types/chat.ts`** (new):
```ts
import type { UIMessage } from "ai"
export type CitationChunk = {
  index: number; id: string; sourceTitle: string; sourceType: string
  url: string | null; positionStart: number | null; pageNumber: number | null; content: string
}
export type ChatMeta = { citations?: CitationChunk[] }
export type ChatMessage = UIMessage<ChatMeta>
```

- **`app/api/chat/route.ts`** (new):
  - Auth check + notebook ownership verification
  - `embedText(userText)` via Voyage AI
  - pgvector search: `db.select(...).from(childChunks).innerJoin(parentChunks).innerJoin(sources).where(...).orderBy(asc(cosineDistance(childChunks.embedding, embedding))).limit(5)`
  - `streamText({ model: anthropic("claude-sonnet-4-6"), system: buildSystemPrompt(citationChunks), messages: await convertToModelMessages(uiMessages), onFinish: async ({ text }) => { /* save user + assistant messages to DB, parse [N] markers, save messageCitations */ } })`
  - Returns `result.toUIMessageStreamResponse<ChatMessage>({ messageMetadata: () => ({ citations: citationChunks }) })`
  - Fixed: removed `.returning()` on user message insert (unused variable lint warning)

- **`app/[locale]/app/[notebookId]/page.tsx`** (modified):
  - Loads messages with full citation JOIN:
```ts
db.select({ id: messages.id, role, content, citationId: messageCitations.childChunkId, citationOrder: messageCitations.orderIndex, positionStart: childChunks.positionStart, pageNumber: childChunks.pageNumber, parentContent: parentChunks.content, sourceTitle: sources.title, sourceType: sources.type, sourceUrl: sources.url })
  .from(messages).leftJoin(messageCitations, ...).leftJoin(childChunks, ...).leftJoin(parentChunks, ...).leftJoin(sources, ...)
  .where(eq(messages.notebookId, notebookId)).orderBy(asc(messages.createdAt), asc(messageCitations.orderIndex))
```
  - Groups by message id, builds `CitationChunk[]` per message
  - Converts to `ChatMessage[]`: `{ id, role, parts: [{ type: "text", text: content, state: "done" }], metadata: citations.length > 0 ? { citations } : undefined }`
  - Passes `<ChatPanel notebookId={notebookId} readySourceCount={readySources.length} initialMessages={initialMessages} />`

- **`components/notebook/chat-panel.tsx`** (major rewrite, current state):
  - Imports: `useChat` from `@ai-sdk/react`, `DefaultChatTransport`/`isTextUIPart` from `ai`, `Markdown` from `react-markdown`, `remarkGfm`, `rehypeRaw`, `HoverCard`/`HoverCardTrigger`/`HoverCardContent` from shadcn, `openSourceView` from `lib/source-view-event`
  - `CitationHoverContent`: 3-section div with Separator, shows source name, content as Markdown, "Quelle anzeigen" button
  - `CitationBadge` with `HoverCard` wrapper using base-ui API:
```tsx
<HoverCard>
  <HoverCardTrigger render={<span className="inline-flex" />} delay={300} closeDelay={100}>
    <button onClick={onClick} className={cn("inline-flex size-[18px] ...", active ? "bg-primary text-primary-foreground" : "bg-primary/10 text-primary hover:bg-primary/20")}>
      {citation.index}
    </button>
  </HoverCardTrigger>
  <HoverCardContent side="top" align="center" className="w-auto p-0">
    <CitationHoverContent citation={citation} />
  </HoverCardContent>
</HoverCard>
```
  - `AssistantContent`: renders with Markdown + rehypeRaw, only converts `[N]` when citations.length > 0
  - `handleCiteClick`: sets activeCitation state + calls `openSourceView(next)`
  - `useChat<ChatMessage>({ transport: new DefaultChatTransport({ api: "/api/chat", body: { notebookId } }), messages: initialMessages })`
  - Footer "Quellen:" row shows ALL unique citations (no expand/collapse — **this is the bug the user reported**)

- **`lib/source-view-event.ts`** (new):
```ts
const EVENT = "yannbooklm:source-view"
export function openSourceView(chunk: CitationChunk) {
  window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent(EVENT, { detail: chunk }))
}
export function closeSourceView() {
  window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent(EVENT, { detail: null }))
}
export function onSourceView(cb: (chunk: CitationChunk | null) => void): () => void {
  const handler = (e: Event) => cb((e as CustomEvent<CitationChunk | null>).detail)
  window.addEventListener(EVENT, handler)
  return () => window.removeEventListener(EVENT, handler)
}
```

- **`components/notebook/source-sidebar.tsx`** (rewritten):
  - Listens for source-view events via `useEffect(() => onSourceView(chunk => { if (chunk) { setCollapsed(false); setActiveChunk(chunk) } else setActiveChunk(null) }), [])`
  - `activeChunk` state: when set, shows `SourceDetailPanel` instead of sources list
  - Current width: `w-72` (288px) — user wants it wider
  - `SourceDetailPanel`: header with `ChevronLeft` back button + source icon + name, scrollable Markdown content, Separator dividers, footer "Quelle anzeigen" link

- **`components/notebook/studio-sidebar.tsx`** (not yet modified):
  - Current width: `w-80` (320px) — user wants it wider

- **`components/ui/hover-card.tsx`** (shadcn-generated, uses `@base-ui/react/preview-card`):
  - `HoverCardTrigger` wraps `PreviewCardPrimitive.Trigger` which has `delay`/`closeDelay` props
  - No `asChild` support → use `render` prop

- **`eslint.config.mjs`** (modified):
  - Added rule `"@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars": ["warn", { varsIgnorePattern: "^_", argsIgnorePattern: "^_" }]` to allow `_node` pattern in react-markdown component handlers

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **`{ Markdown }` named import from react-markdown**: react-markdown v10 only exports default. Fixed: `import Markdown from "react-markdown"`
- **Implicit `any` in react-markdown component handlers**: Cannot destructure `({ children })` directly in component handlers — TypeScript can't infer. Fixed: use `function p(props) { const { node: _node, children, ...rest } = props; return ... }` pattern
- **`_node` unused variable lint warnings**: Added `varsIgnorePattern: "^_"` to ESLint config
- **HoverCard API mismatch**: shadcn's hover-card uses base-ui `PreviewCard` not Radix. `openDelay`/`closeDelay` don't exist on Root, `asChild` doesn't exist on Trigger. Fixed: remove `openDelay`/`closeDelay` from `<HoverCard>`, use `delay`/`closeDelay` on `<HoverCardTrigger>`, use `render={<span className="inline-flex" />}` instead of `asChild`
- **[N] badges disappearing for historical messages**: When `initialMessages` had no `metadata.citations`, the `cite` component returned `null` for all badges, making them invisible. Fixed: only replace `[N]` with `<cite>` tags when `citations.length > 0`, otherwise pass text as-is
- **Unused `userRow` lint warning in route**: Changed to `await db.insert(...).values(...)` without `.returning()`
- **Chat history empty on load**: Messages saved to DB but `useChat` started empty. Fixed: load messages from DB in `page.tsx`, pass as `initialMessages` prop

5. Problem Solving:
- pgvector search without raw SQL: discovered `cosineDistance()` from drizzle-orm accepts `number[]` directly — no raw SQL needed
- AI SDK v6 `useChat` not in `ai` or `ai/react`: found it in `@ai-sdk/react` (separate package, had to npm install)
- Citation data to client: used `messageMetadata` option on `toUIMessageStreamResponse` which sends data in stream; accessible as `msg.metadata?.citations` on each message in `useChat`
- Sibling component communication (ChatPanel → SourceSidebar): used custom window events (`CustomEvent`) since both are client components but mounted in different subtrees (layout vs page)

7. Pending Tasks:

## 🤖 Assistant

Klarer Befund. Zwei separate Probleme:

**Problem 1 — Bilder werden gar nicht extrahiert:** `parsePdf()` ruft nur `getTextContent()` auf. `PdfPage` hat nur ein `text`-Feld, kein `images`.

**Problem 2 — JPEG 2000 nicht dekodierbar:** Fast alle Bilder im Kreta-PDF sind im JPX/JP2-Format (JPEG 2000). pdfjs-dist benötigt dafür WebAssembly (`wasmUrl`-Parameter) — das ist in der aktuellen Node.js-Konfiguration nicht eingerichtet. Daher `JpxError: OpenJPEG failed to initialize`.

Page 1 hat **58 Image-Ops**, Page 2 hat **57** — alle scheitern am JPX-Decoder.

---

Für die **Sidebar-Ansicht** gibt es zwei realistische Lösungswege:

---

## 🤖 Assistant

*[Tool: `AskUserQuestion`]*
  - `questions`: [{'question': 'Wie soll der Quellen-Inhalt in der Sidebar dargestellt werden?', 'header': 'PDF-Anzeige', 'options': [{'l

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Das WebAssembly-Problem ist lösbar — aber es löst nur **die Hälfte** des Problems:

**Was WebAssembly (wasmUrl für openjpeg) löst:**
pdfjs kann JPEG-2000-Bilder dekodieren → die 58 JPX-Fehler pro Seite wären weg.

**Was es nicht löst:**
pdfjs hat **keine Node.js-API um dekodierten Image-Pixel-Data auszulesen**. Es ist ein Renderer, kein Extraktor. Um Bild-Bytes herauszubekommen bräuchte man zusätzlich eine Canvas-Implementierung (`node-canvas` oder `@napi-rs/canvas`), auf die pdfjs rendert — dann kann man das Canvas als PNG auslesen.

**Kürzerer Weg:** `mupdf.js` — ein nativer Node.js-Wrapper für die MuPDF-Bibliothek. Kann Text + Bilder extrahieren, kennt JPEG 2000 nativ, ohne WebAssembly-Fummelei. Oder: Für die Sidebar-Vorschau einfach die PDF per `<iframe>` aus S3 einbetten — dann braucht man überhaupt keine Extraction für die Anzeige.

---

ARGUMENTS: Layout der Quellenvorschau-Sidebar (SourceDetailPanel) mit PDF-Bildunterstützung via mupdf.js. Kontext: PDFs werden aktuell nur als Text-Chunks gespeichert (keine Bilder). mupdf kann Seiten als PNG rendern (page.toPixmap().asPNG()) oder einzelne Bilder extrahieren (walk() → image.toPixmap()). Die Bilder müssen irgendwo gespeichert werden (S3) und in der Sidebar angezeigt werden.
