This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

**Issue #9 (Notes) completion tasks:**
- 50:50 layout: note editor and chat should share space equally, not hide chat
- Empty text source validation: content must have ≥ 5 chars to enable "Als Quelle festlegen"
- Character count display: `0/5` (red below min), `n/10.000` (gray above min)
- Note dropdown menu: icons + whitespace-nowrap + w-max + correct positioning
- Source sidebar dropdown: same w-max + whitespace-nowrap
- E2E tests: create note, delete note, set as source + delete source
- Issue #9 closure (chat export + GitHub close)

**Issue #14 (Quiz):**
- Grilling session before implementation
- Einmalig generiert und gespeichert as StudioOutput
- Clicking "Quiz" in tools grid ALWAYS generates new quiz (new StudioOutput)
- One question at a time, A/B/C/D + "Überspringen" button (direct skip without answering)
- Sofort-Feedback per answer (green/red + explanation)
- Ergebnisscreen: circle progress (SVG), Richtig/Falsch/Übersprungen stats, Behandelte Themen, Weiterlernen topic chips + "Quiz erstellen" (overwrites current output), "Quiz noch einmal ansehen" (review mode), "Quiz wiederholen" (fresh restart)
- Review mode: correct answers pre-highlighted with "Richtige Antwort(übersprungen)" label
- StudioSidebar (not fullscreen), 50:50 split with chat
- JSON stored in `studioOutputs.data`: `{ questions: [{question, options: string[], correct: number, explanation}][], topics: string[], suggestions: string[] }`
- Context-aware dropdown: quiz items → Teilen/Umbenennen/Prompt ansehen/Löschen; note items → existing menu

**Rename feature (current):**
- "Umbenennen" from quiz dropdown → small inline dialog in list entry
- `title` column added to `studioOutputs` table
- PATCH endpoint to update title
- `studioOutputToItem` uses `o.title ?? tStudio(o.type)` as fallback

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- Next.js App Router with Server Components + Client Components
- Drizzle ORM with `drizzle-kit push` for schema migrations
- `generateObject` from Vercel AI SDK with Zod schema for structured Claude output
- `pgEnum` for `studioOutputTypeEnum` (audio, mindmap, slidedeck, datatable, quiz)
- `jsonb` column for storing quiz data
- Playwright E2E tests with `fixtures.local.ts` (JWT cookie injection)
- `next-intl` for i18n (useTranslations, useLocale)
- Radix UI Dialog, DropdownMenu components (shadcn/ui)
- TipTap editor (ProseMirror) for note content
- GIS (Google Identity Services) for OAuth popup (drive.file, spreadsheets scopes)
- SVG circle progress indicator (manual calculation)
- React state machine for quiz (quiz/review/results modes)

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`components/notebook/notebook-shell.tsx`**
  - Changed `<main>` from `noteMode ? "w-0" : "flex-1"` to always `flex-1`
  - Removed unused `cn` import
  ```tsx
  <main className="flex flex-1 overflow-hidden">
  ```

- **`components/notebook/studio-sidebar.tsx`** (major changes throughout session)
  - Added `studioOutputsList` state (was using `initialStudioOutputs` directly)
  - Added `activeQuiz`, `quizLoading`, `renamingOutput`, `renameValue` state
  - Updated `studioOutputToItem` to use `o.title ?? tStudio(o.type)`
  - Added `openQuiz()`, `closeQuiz()`, `generateQuiz()`, `handleRenameStudioOutput()`, `handleDeleteStudioOutput()`
  - Context-aware dropdown: quiz items get Teilen/Umbenennen/Prompt/Löschen; note items keep existing menu
  - Quiz view renders in sidebar like note detail (same 50:50 split via `onNoteModeChange(true)`)
  - Rename dialog added at end of return wrapped in `<>...</>`
  - Character count in note footer: `{belowMin ? `${len}/5` : `${len.toLocaleString()}/10.000`}`
  - Trash button in note title bar: `aria-label={t("deleteNote")}`
  - Important: `studioOutputToItem` fallback: `o.title ?? tStudio(o.type)`

- **`components/notebook/quiz-view.tsx`** (NEW)
  - Full quiz UI: question-by-question, progress bar, options (A-D), skip button, feedback, results screen
  - `Mode = "quiz" | "review" | "results"`
  - `AnswerState = { selected: number | null; skipped: boolean }`
  - `CircleProgress` component with SVG
  - Review mode: `getOptionClass()` shows correct answer in green, others dimmed
  - Results screen: circle + stats + Behandelte Themen + Weiterlernen chips + "Quiz erstellen" button
  - Props: `data: QuizData, outputId: string, onNewQuizFromTopic: (topic, outputId) => void`

- **`app/api/studio/quiz/route.ts`** (NEW)
  - POST endpoint with `generateObject` + Zod schema (15 questions, topics, suggestions)
  - Accepts optional `focusTopic` and `outputId` (to overwrite existing)
  - `getSourceContent()`: fetches up to 60 parent chunks from ready+enabled sources
  - Returns new or updated StudioOutput

- **`app/api/studio/quiz/[id]/route.ts`** (NEW)
  - PATCH: updates `title` field on studioOutput (rename)
  - DELETE: removes studioOutput (with ownership check via notebooks table)

- **`db/schema.ts`**
  - Added `title: text("title")` to `studioOutputs` table (nullable)
  - Migration applied via `drizzle-kit push`

- **`messages/de.json`** and **`messages/en.json`**
  - Added under `studio`: `quizRegenerate`, `quizGenerating`, `quizNoSources`, `quizError`, `share`, `rename`, `viewPrompt`, `delete`
  - **MISSING** (causing current errors): `studio.cancel`, `studio.save`, `studio.renameError`

- **`tests/notes.local.spec.ts`** (NEW)
  - 3 tests using `fixtures.local.ts`, all passing in 22s
  - Helpers: `createNotebook()`, `tryDeleteNotebook()`, `studioSidebar = page.locator("aside").last()`, `sourceSidebar = page.locator("aside").first()`
  - Test 1: Click FAB → expect title input visible
  - Test 2: Click FAB → click Trash button (aria-label="Löschen") → expect "Noch keine Ergebnisse"
  - Test 3: Create note, type content in `.ProseMirror`, wait 2.5s, click "Als Quelle festlegen", verify source in left aside, delete source

- **`lib/google-docs-export.ts`**
  - `exportNoteToGoogleDocs()`: GIS popup (drive.file scope) → marked MD→HTML → Drive multipart upload
  - `exportNoteToGoogleSheets()`: GIS popup (spreadsheets scope) → parse MD tables → Sheets API
  - `parseMarkdownTables()`: GFM table parser → `string[][][]`
  - `getAccessToken(clientId, scope)`: refactored to accept scope parameter

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **`createdAt.getTime is not a function`**: API JSON response has `createdAt` as string, not Date. Fixed by casting and converting: `const output = { ...raw, createdAt: new Date(raw.createdAt) }`

- **`Bookmarks` not exported from lucide-react**: Replaced with `Library` icon

- **`studio_DUPLICATE_REMOVE` accidentally created**: When adding studio keys, accidentally created a duplicate namespace entry. Fixed by reading the file and removing the spurious block.

- **JSX must have one parent element**: `return (<aside>...</aside><Dialog>...)` — fixed by wrapping in `<>...</>`

- **MISSING_MESSAGE studio.cancel / studio.save**: Not yet fixed — this is the current error. Needs to be added to both `messages/de.json` and `messages/en.json` under `studio` namespace. Also `studio.renameError` is referenced in code.

- **`Bookmarks` icon**: doesn't exist in lucide-react → replaced with `Library`

- **`w-0` hiding chat**: Changed to always `flex-1` so note editor shares space 50:50

- **JSON parse error in de.json**: Previous session issue with unicode quotes in i18n values

- **Background task hung**: First `npx playwright test` background run got stuck; ran synchronously instead → 3/3 passed

5. Problem Solving:

- **50:50 split**: Both `<main>` and StudioSidebar get `flex-1` when noteMode is true — achieved by removing the conditional `w-0` class from `<main>`
- **Quiz state machine**: Three modes (quiz/review/results) managed in `QuizView` component; answers tracked as `AnswerState[]` array indexed by question number
- **Multiple quizzes**: `studioOutputsList` state (not `initialStudioOutputs`) allows dynamic additions without page reload
- **"Quiz erstellen" from Weiterlernen**: Calls `onNewQuizFromTopic(topic, outputId)` which calls `generateQuiz(topic, outputId)` → PATCH overwrites current StudioOutput's data
- **Rename UX**: Small inline Dialog (not full modal) with Input + Enter key support + Save/Cancel buttons

## 🧑 User

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/tdd

# Test-Driven Development

## Philosophy

**Core principle**: Tests should verify behavior through public interfaces, not implementation details. Code can change entirely; tests shouldn't.

**Good tests** are integration-style: they exercise real code paths through public APIs. They describe _what_ the system does, not _how_ it does it. A good test reads like a specification - "user can checkout with valid cart" tells you exactly what capability exists. These tests survive refactors because they don't care about internal structure.

**Bad tests** are coupled to implementation. They mock internal collaborators, test private methods, or verify through external means (like querying a database directly instead of using the interface). The warning sign: your test breaks when you refactor, but behavior hasn't changed. If you rename an internal function and tests fail, those tests were testing implementation, not behavior.

See [tests.md](tests.md) for examples and [mocking.md](mocking.md) for mocking guidelines.

## Anti-Pattern: Horizontal Slices

**DO NOT write all tests first, then all implementation.** This is "horizontal slicing" - treating RED as "write all tests" and GREEN as "write all code."

This produces **crap tests**:

- Tests written in bulk test _imagined_ behavior, not _actual_ behavior
- You end up testing the _shape_ of things (data structures, function signatures) rather than user-facing behavior
- Tests become insensitive to real changes - they pass when behavior breaks, fail when behavior is fine
- You outrun your headlights, committing to test structure before understanding the implementation

**Correct approach**: Vertical slices via tracer bullets. One test → one implementation → repeat. Each test responds to what you learned from the previous cycle. Because you just wrote the code, you know exactly what behavior matters and how to verify it.

```
WRONG (horizontal):
  RED:   test1, test2, test3, test4, test5
  GREEN: impl1, impl2, impl3, impl4, impl5

RIGHT (vertical):
  RED→GREEN: test1→impl1
  RED→GREEN: test2→impl2
  RED→GREEN: test3→impl3
  ...
```

## Workflow

### 1. Planning

When exploring the codebase, read `CONTEXT.md` (if it exists) so that test names and interface vocabulary match the project's domain language, and respect ADRs in the area you're touching.

Before writing any code:

- [ ] Confirm with user what interface changes are needed
- [ ] Confirm with user which behaviors to test (prioritize)
- [ ] Identify opportunities for deep modules (small interface, deep implementation) — run the `/codebase-design` skill for the vocabulary and the testability checks
- [ ] List the behaviors to test (not implementation steps)
- [ ] Get user approval on the plan

Ask: "What should the public interface look like? Which behaviors are most important to test?"

**You can't test everything.** Confirm with the user exactly which behaviors matter most. Focus testing effort on critical paths and complex logic, not every possible edge case.

### 2. Tracer Bullet

Write ONE test that confirms ONE thing about the system:

```
RED:   Write test for first behavior → test fails
GREEN: Write minimal code to pass → test passes
```

This is your tracer bullet - proves the path works end-to-end.

### 3. Incremental Loop

For each remaining behavior:

```
RED:   Write next test → fails
GREEN: Minimal code to pass → passes
```

Rules:

- One test at a time
- Only enough code to pass current test
- Don't anticipate future tests
- Keep tests focused on observable behavior

### 4. Refactor

After all tests pass, look for [refactor candidates](refactoring.md):

- [ ] Extract duplication
- [ ] Deepen modules (move complexity behind simple interfaces)
- [ ] Apply SOLID principles where natural
- [ ] Consider what new code reveals about existing code
- [ ] Run tests after each refactor step

**Never refactor while RED.** Get to GREEN first.

## Checklist Per Cycle

```
[ ] Test describes behavior, not implementation
[ ] Test uses public interface only
[ ] Test would survive internal refactor
[ ] Code is minimal for this test
[ ] No speculative features added
```


ARGUMENTS: Playwright E2E tests für Quiz-Feature: Quiz erstellen (Klick auf Quiz-Button im Tools-Grid → Spinner erscheint in Ergebnisliste), Quiz umbenennen (Drei-Punkte-Menü → Umbenennen → Inline-Dialog → Speichern), Quiz löschen (Drei-Punkte-Menü → Löschen), Modal "Prompt und Quellen ansehen" öffnen (Quellen-Chips sichtbar), Klick auf ↺ → Anpassen-Ansicht im Modal sichtbar, dann gemockter Klick auf "Generieren". Bestehende Playwright-Infrastruktur mit JWT-Session-Fixtures vorhanden (fixtures.local.ts). Vorhandene Tests in tests/notes.local.spec.ts als Referenz nutzen. WICHTIG: Quiz-Generierung ist API-abhängig (Claude API) — der "Erstellen"-Test soll nur prüfen ob der Spinner erscheint, nicht das fertige Quiz. Den Generieren-Klick im Modal per Route-Mock abfangen.

## 🧑 User

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   **Issue #14 (Quiz) completion tasks** — Started the session fixing missing i18n keys (`studio.cancel`, `studio.save`) by switching to `tCommon`. Then implemented a series of features:
   - **Share (Teilen)**: Magic link via UUID token stored in `studioOutputs.share_token`, public route `/share/[token]` (no auth), Web Share API with clipboard fallback
   - **Public quiz adjustments**: `readOnly` prop on QuizView hides "Quiz erstellen" + Weiterlernen section; stats no longer reset on "Quiz noch einmal ansehen"; full-width centered layout on share page; circular SVG progress ring on question screen; fixed SVG ring visibility using Tailwind `stroke-*` classes
   - **Quiz Sources Modal**: "Prompt und Quellen ansehen" opens a two-view modal (sources chips / customize view with count 10/15/25, difficulty einfach/mittel/schwierig, focusTopic textarea). Modal uses `sm:max-w-2xl`, buttons are `items-start` auto-width. ↺ button at `absolute right-12 top-4`
   - **Quiz API**: Stores `usedSources: { id, title, type }[]` in data JSON; accepts `count` and `difficulty` params; `difficultyContext` map sends narrative per selected level only
   - **Spinner in results list**: Loading placeholder (Loader2) for new quizzes; `regeneratingOutputId` state for regenerating existing ones
   - **Source chip click**: Closes modal, opens source detail panel in left sidebar via custom DOM event `yannbooklm:open-source`
   - **E2E Tests**: `tests/quiz.local.spec.ts` with 5 tests covering all above flows using mocked API routes

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js App Router with middleware for auth bypass (`isSharePage` early return)
   - Drizzle ORM + `drizzle-kit push` (migration via raw SQL when TTY unavailable)
   - `generateObject` from Vercel AI SDK with dynamic Zod schema (`questions.length(count)`)
   - `crypto.randomUUID()` for share tokens
   - Web Share API (`navigator.share`) with clipboard fallback
   - Radix UI Dialog, DropdownMenu (shadcn/ui); `data-slot="dropdown-menu-trigger"`
   - SVG circle progress (manual dasharray calculation); Tailwind `stroke-*` utility classes
   - Custom DOM events (`yannbooklm:open-source`) for cross-component communication
   - `useRef` pattern for calling functions defined after `useEffect`
   - Playwright `page.route()` for API mocking in E2E tests

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`components/notebook/studio-sidebar.tsx`**
     - Added `tCommon = useTranslations("common")` for cancel/save
     - Added `newQuizLoading`, `regeneratingOutputId`, `sourcesModalOutput` states
     - `generateQuiz(focusTopic?, outputId?, count?, difficulty?)` — passes new params
     - Loading placeholder in `outputs` array: `{ id: "__loading__", kind: "quiz", loading: true }`
     - Spinner icon `Loader2` when `item.loading || item.id === regeneratingOutputId`
     - DropdownMenu wrapped in `{!item.loading && (...)}` 
     - "Prompt und Quellen ansehen" wired to `setSourcesModalOutput(studioOutputsList.find(o => o.id === item.id))`
     - `<QuizSourcesModal>` added at end of return
     - Share button calls `POST /api/studio/quiz/[id]/share` → gets token → `navigator.share` or clipboard
   
   - **`components/notebook/quiz-view.tsx`**
     - Added `QuizUsedSource` type: `{ id: string; title: string; type: string }`
     - Extended `QuizData`: `usedSources?: QuizUsedSource[]`
     - Added `readOnly?: boolean` prop
     - `QuizCircleProgress` component replacing linear bar on question screen (size-16, r=28)
     - `CircleProgress`: stroke uses `className="stroke-muted-foreground/25"` and `className="stroke-primary"`
     - `restart(reviewMode)`: only resets answers when `!reviewMode`
     - Results screen: `cn(..., readOnly && "items-center")` on outer div
     - Weiterlernen section: wrapped in `{!readOnly && (...)}`
     - Grid: `readOnly ? "flex flex-col gap-4" : "grid grid-cols-2 gap-4"`
     - Buttons row: `cn("flex gap-2 border-t pt-4", readOnly && "justify-center")`

   - **`components/notebook/quiz-sources-modal.tsx`** (NEW)
     ```tsx
     type View = "sources" | "customize"
     type Difficulty = "einfach" | "mittel" | "schwierig"
     
     // Source icons by type
     const SOURCE_ICONS: Record<string, React.ReactNode> = {
       pdf: <FileText .../>,
       url: <Globe .../>,
       youtube: <FaYoutube .../>,
       text: <AlignLeft />,
     }
     
     const COUNT_OPTIONS = [
       { label: "Weniger", value: 10 },
       { label: "Standard (Standardeinstellung)", value: 15 },
       { label: "Mehr", value: 25 },
     ]
     const DIFFICULTY_OPTIONS = [
       { label: "Einfach", value: "einfach" },
       { label: "Mittel (Standardeinstellung)", value: "mittel" },
       { label: "Schwierig", value: "schwierig" },
     ]
     
     // ↺ button: absolute right-12 top-4 to avoid overlapping Dialog X
     // Sources: clickable buttons → onOpenChange(false); openSourceById(s.id)
     // Customize: items-start auto-width buttons
     // Generieren: onGenerate({ count, difficulty, focusTopic }) → closes modal
     ```

   - **`app/api/studio/quiz/route.ts`**
     - `type Difficulty = "einfach" | "mittel" | "schwierig"`
     - `difficultyContext: Record<Difficulty, string>` — narrative per level
     - `getSourceContent()` returns `{ content, usedSources: { id, title, type }[] }`
     - Dynamic schema: `z.array(...).length(count)`
     - Prompt: `Schwierigkeitsgrad: ${difficulty} – ${difficultyContext[difficulty]}`
     - Stores `{ ...object, usedSources }` in data

   - **`app/api/studio/quiz/[id]/share/route.ts`** (NEW)
     - POST: generates/returns UUID share token (idempotent, checks ownership via notebooks join)

   - **`app/api/studio/quiz/[id]/route.ts`**
     - PATCH: updates `title`; DELETE: removes output (with ownership check)

   - **`app/share/[token]/page.tsx`** (NEW)
     - Public page, no auth, loads quiz by shareToken, renders SharedQuizClient
     - `max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 py-6`

   - **`app/share/[token]/shared-quiz-client.tsx`** (NEW)
     - `"use client"` wrapper: `<QuizView data={data} outputId={outputId} onNewQuizFromTopic={() => undefined} readOnly />`

   - **`middleware.ts`**
     - Added `const isSharePage = pathname.startsWith("/share/")`
     - Early return: `if (isSharePage) return NextResponse.next()`

   - **`db/schema.ts`**
     - Added `shareToken: text("share_token").unique()` to studioOutputs
     - Migration via raw SQL: `ALTER TABLE studio_outputs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS share_token TEXT UNIQUE;`

   - **`lib/source-view-event.ts`**
     - Added `openSourceById(sourceId: string)` dispatching `yannbooklm:open-source` event
     - Added `onOpenSourceById(cb)` listener

   - **`components/notebook/source-sidebar.tsx`**
     - Added `handleSourceClickRef = useRef<...>()` 
     - `useEffect` with `onOpenSourceById` → finds source by ID → calls `handleSourceClickRef.current(source)`
     - `useEffect(() => { handleSourceClickRef.current = handleSourceClick })` to keep ref current
     - Source list items: added `id={`source-${source.id}`}`

   - **`messages/de.json`** and **`messages/en.json`**
     - Added `studio.renameError`, `studio.shareCopied`, `studio.shareError`

   - **`tests/quiz.local.spec.ts`** (NEW — currently running)
     ```ts
     const MOCK_QUIZ_ID = "mock-quiz-e2e-abc123"
     const MOCK_QUIZ_OUTPUT = { id: MOCK_QUIZ_ID, type: "quiz", data: { questions: [...], usedSources: [{ id: "src-001", title: "Mathe-Buch.pdf", type: "pdf" }] } }
     
     // 5 tests:
     // 1. Spinner: mock with 4s delay, click Quiz button, expect animate-spin
     // 2. Rename: mock POST + PATCH, createMockQuizAndWait, hover → dropdown-trigger → Umbenennen → fill → Speichern → check "Mein Quiz"
     // 3. Delete: mock POST + DELETE 204, dropdown → Löschen → expect "Noch keine Ergebnisse"
     // 4. Modal Quellen: mock POST, open dropdown → "Prompt und Quellen ansehen" → expect "Mathe-Buch.pdf"
     // 5. Anpassen: open modal → click "Quiz anpassen" → check "Anzahl der Fragen" visible → mock POST → click "Generieren" → expect dialog gone
     ```

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **`studio.cancel`/`studio.save` MISSING_MESSAGE**: Fixed by adding `tCommon = useTranslations("common")` and using `tCommon("cancel")` / `tCommon("save")`
   - **`drizzle-kit push` needs TTY** for unique constraint prompt: Fixed by running raw SQL `ALTER TABLE studio_outputs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS share_token TEXT UNIQUE;`
   - **`Textarea` not in shadcn/ui**: Used native `<textarea>` with Tailwind classes
   - **`Youtube` not in lucide-react**: Used `FaYoutube` from `react-icons/fa`
   - **`output` not in scope** in dropdown click handler: Used `studioOutputsList.find((o) => o.id === item.id)`
   - **JSX parsing error** `{!item.loading && <DropdownMenu>...</DropdownMenu>}`: Fixed by wrapping closing tag correctly with `{!item.loading && (<DropdownMenu>...</DropdownMenu>)}`
   - **SVG ring invisible** on dark background: `hsl(var(--muted))` too dark → switched to Tailwind `stroke-muted-foreground/25` and `stroke-primary`
   - **`handleSourceClickRef.current = handleSourceClick` during render**: Fixed by wrapping in `useEffect(() => { handleSourceClickRef.current = handleSourceClick })`
   - **`[cannot access refs during render]`**: Same as above

5. Problem Solving:
   - Share token idempotency: `if (output.shareToken) return NextResponse.json({ token: output.shareToken })` before generating new one
   - Dynamic Zod schema for variable question count: moved `QuizSchema` inside `POST` handler where `count` is available
   - Buttons in customize modal too wide: changed to `flex flex-col items-start gap-2` instead of `flex flex-col gap-2`
   - ↺ button overlapping Dialog's X: positioned `absolute right-12 top-4` to be left of the Dialog's own X at `right-4 top-4`
   - Cross-component navigation (quiz modal → source sidebar): custom DOM event `yannbooklm:open-source` dispatched from modal, listened in source sidebar

## 🧑 User

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/dev-server

# Dev Server — Start & Monitor

Start the Next.js dev server and stream errors from its log file.

## Steps

### 1. Kill any existing server on port 3000

```bash
fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
```

### 2. Start the server using Bash run_in_background

Use the Bash tool with `run_in_background: true`. Save the background task ID — the output file path is needed for the Monitor in step 4.

Load the correct Node version via nvm before starting:

```
Bash(
  command:           "fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1; source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use && npm run dev",
  run_in_background: true
)
```

The tool returns a task ID and output path like:
`/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/<id>.output`

Save this path — it is used in step 4.

### 3. Wait until the server responds

```bash
until curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000 | grep -qE "200|302|307"; do sleep 1; done && echo "Server ready"
```

### 4. Start the persistent Monitor

Filter the log for errors and warnings only — do not emit every line.

```
Monitor(
  command:     "tail -f <output-path-from-step-2> 2>&1 | grep -E --line-buffered -i 'error|warn|failed|unhandled|exception|TypeError|ReferenceError|SyntaxError|cannot'",
  description: "Next.js dev server errors and warnings",
  persistent:  true
)
```

## On error notification

When the Monitor fires:

1. Read the last 60 lines of the log for full context:
   ```bash
   tail -60 .next/dev/logs/next-development.log
   ```
2. Identify root cause (missing table → DrizzleAdapter; JWEInvalid → session strategy mismatch; 42P01 → wrong table name, etc.)
3. Fix the code, verify with `npm run typecheck && npm run lint`
4. Inform the user of what was fixed.

## Important rules

- **Never print .env values** — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- Do not use `&` in Bash tool calls — use `run_in_background: true` or the Node spawn approach above
- `nohup ... &` and `... &` both fail with exit code 144 in this environment — always use the Node spawn detach pattern

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Der Skill ist unter `~/.claude/skills/dev-agent/SKILL.md` installiert und sofort verfügbar.

**Verwendung:**
```
/dev-agent backend /tmp/tasks.log
```

**Ablauf:**
1. Startet einen persistenten Monitor auf die Log-Datei, gefiltert auf `[Task] Agent backend: `
2. Wenn eine Zeile matcht, extrahiert er die Task-Beschreibung
3. Startet eine Grilling-Session (eine Frage nach der anderen) um die Anforderungen zu klären
4. Implementiert die Lösung nach abgeschlossenem Grilling
5. Überwacht weiter auf den nächsten Task

Das Log-Format muss dabei `[Task] Agent <name>: <beschreibung>` sein.

## 🧑 User

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/dev-server

# Dev Server — Start & Monitor

Start the Next.js dev server and stream errors from its log file.

## Steps

### 1. Kill any existing server on port 3000

```bash
fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
```

### 2. Start the server using Bash run_in_background

Use the Bash tool with `run_in_background: true`. Save the background task ID — the output file path is needed for the Monitor in step 4.

Load the correct Node version via nvm before starting:

```
Bash(
  command:           "fuser -k 3000/tcp 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1; source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use && npm run dev",
  run_in_background: true
)
```

The tool returns a task ID and output path like:
`/tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/<id>.output`

Save this path — it is used in step 4.

### 3. Wait until the server responds

```bash
until curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000 | grep -qE "200|302|307"; do sleep 1; done && echo "Server ready"
```

### 4. Print the output path

```bash
echo "To create /dev-agents use the path <output-path-from-step-2>"
```

### 4. Start the persistent Monitor

Filter the log for errors and warnings only — do not emit every line.

```
Monitor(
  command:     "tail -f <output-path-from-step-2> 2>&1 | grep -E --line-buffered -i 'error|warn|failed|unhandled|exception|TypeError|ReferenceError|SyntaxError|cannot'",
  description: "Next.js dev server errors and warnings",
  persistent:  true
)
```

## On error notification

When the Monitor fires:

1. Read the last 60 lines of the log for full context:
   ```bash
   tail -60 .next/dev/logs/next-development.log
   ```
2. Identify root cause (missing table → DrizzleAdapter; JWEInvalid → session strategy mismatch; 42P01 → wrong table name, etc.)
3. Fix the code, verify with `npm run typecheck && npm run lint`
4. Inform the user of what was fixed.

## Important rules

- **Never print .env values** — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`
- Do not use `&` in Bash tool calls — use `run_in_background: true` or the Node spawn approach above
- `nohup ... &` and `... &` both fail with exit code 144 in this environment — always use the Node spawn detach pattern

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Gut, ich hab den Kontext. Los geht's.

---

**Wie soll eine Karteikarte aussehen?**

Beim Quiz haben wir Frage + 4 Antwortoptionen + Erklärung. Bei Karteikarten gibt es verschiedene Modelle:

- **Vorderseite / Rückseite** — klassisch: Begriff vorne, Definition hinten
- **Frage / Antwort** — wie Quiz, aber ohne Optionen, Freitext-Recall
- **Cloze (Lückentext)** — „Die Hauptstadt von Frankreich ist ___"

Meine Empfehlung: **Vorderseite / Rückseite**, da das das intuitivste Modell ist und gut mit `generateObject` abbildbar. Frage/Antwort wäre fast identisch aber klingt weniger nach Karteikarte.

Was bevorzugst du?

---

## 🧑 User

Vorderseite / Rückseite

---

## 🤖 Assistant

**Wie soll das Lernen mit den Karten ablaufen?**

Beim Lernen gibt es zwei gängige Ansätze:

- **Einfaches Durchblättern** — Karte zeigen, Rückseite aufdecken, selbst einschätzen ob man es wusste (z.B. "Wusste ich" / "Wusste ich nicht")
- **Spaced Repetition (SM-2 o.ä.)** — Karten werden nach Schwierigkeitsgrad priorisiert, bekannte Karten seltener wiederholt

Meine Empfehlung: **Einfaches Durchblättern** für den ersten Slice. Spaced Repetition ist ein großes eigenes System (Daten persistieren, Scheduling-Algorithmus) — das kann immer noch als V2 drauf gesetzt werden.

---

## 🧑 User

Es werden 50 Lernkarten erstellt, die wie im layout auf dem screenshot aufgebaut sind.  Verwende Einfaches Durchblättern

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Sehr klar, danke für den Screenshot. Ich sehe: Vorderseite → "Antwort ansehen" → Rückseite aufdecken → selbst mit ✗ oder ✓ bewerten, Zähler ✗ 0 / 1 ✓ läuft mit, Pfeile zum Navigieren.

---

**Was passiert wenn man die letzte Karte bewertet hat — gibt es einen Abschluss-Screen?**

Beim Quiz gibt es eine Ergebnisseite (X/15, 53% etc.) mit der Option „Noch einmal". Optionen für Karteikarten:

- **Ergebnis-Screen** — zeigt ✓/✗-Verhältnis, Option „Falsche Karten wiederholen" und „Neu starten"
- **Einfach zur ersten Karte zurück** — kein Summary, Loop

Meine Empfehlung: **Ergebnis-Screen** mit der Option, nur die falsch beantworteten Karten zu wiederholen — das ist der natürliche nächste Schritt nach dem Durchlauf und deutlich wertvoller als ein stiller Loop.

---

**Was zeigt "Vollständigen Bericht anzeigen"?**

In NotebookLM öffnet das vermutlich eine Detailansicht aller Karten mit ihrem jeweiligen Status. Optionen:

- **Liste aller Karten** mit Vorder-/Rückseite und ✓/✗/→ Status
- **Nur die falsch beantworteten** als kompakte Übersicht
- **Nichts davon für V1** — Button weglassen, "Nochmal üben" reicht

Meine Empfehlung: **Für V1 weglassen**. Der Bericht ist nice-to-have, macht den Scope aber deutlich größer. "Nochmal üben" mit den Optionen „Alle Karten" / „Falsche Karten wiederholen" deckt den wichtigsten Use-Case ab.

---

**Soll die Karte beim Aufdecken der Antwort animiert umklappen (CSS flip)?**

Klassische Karteikarten klappen physisch um. Optionen:

- **CSS 3D-Flip** — Karte dreht sich um die Y-Achse, Rückseite erscheint
- **Kein Flip** — Antwort blendet einfach ein / ersetzt den Text

Meine Empfehlung: **CSS 3D-Flip**. Das ist ikonisch für Karteikarten, technisch einfach umsetzbar (`perspective` + `rotateY`) und macht die Interaktion deutlich befriedigender als ein einfaches Einblenden.

**Soll es beim Generieren Anpassungsoptionen geben — wie beim Quiz (Anzahl, Schwierigkeit, Fokusthema)?**

Beim Quiz kann man vor dem Generieren 10/15/25 Fragen, Schwierigkeitsgrad und ein Fokusthema wählen. Für Karteikarten wären denkbar:

- **Anzahl** — du sagst 50, aber vielleicht auch 25 / 50 / 100 wählbar?
- **Fokusthema** — "Erstelle Karten nur zum Thema Reiselogistik"
- **Schwierigkeit** — eher untypisch für Karteikarten, da kein Multiple-Choice

Meine Empfehlung: **Anzahl (25/50/100) + Fokusthema**, kein Schwierigkeitsgrad. Das Modal wäre identisch mit dem Quiz-Modal, minus Difficulty. Standard auf 50.

Base directory for this skill: /home/homior/.claude/skills/tdd

# Test-Driven Development

## Philosophy

**Core principle**: Tests should verify behavior through public interfaces, not implementation details. Code can change entirely; tests shouldn't.

**Good tests** are integration-style: they exercise real code paths through public APIs. They describe _what_ the system does, not _how_ it does it. A good test reads like a specification - "user can checkout with valid cart" tells you exactly what capability exists. These tests survive refactors because they don't care about internal structure.

**Bad tests** are coupled to implementation. They mock internal collaborators, test private methods, or verify through external means (like querying a database directly instead of using the interface). The warning sign: your test breaks when you refactor, but behavior hasn't changed. If you rename an internal function and tests fail, those tests were testing implementation, not behavior.

See [tests.md](tests.md) for examples and [mocking.md](mocking.md) for mocking guidelines.

## Anti-Pattern: Horizontal Slices

**DO NOT write all tests first, then all implementation.** This is "horizontal slicing" - treating RED as "write all tests" and GREEN as "write all code."

This produces **crap tests**:

- Tests written in bulk test _imagined_ behavior, not _actual_ behavior
- You end up testing the _shape_ of things (data structures, function signatures) rather than user-facing behavior
- Tests become insensitive to real changes - they pass when behavior breaks, fail when behavior is fine
- You outrun your headlights, committing to test structure before understanding the implementation

**Correct approach**: Vertical slices via tracer bullets. One test → one implementation → repeat. Each test responds to what you learned from the previous cycle. Because you just wrote the code, you know exactly what behavior matters and how to verify it.

```
WRONG (horizontal):
  RED:   test1, test2, test3, test4, test5
  GREEN: impl1, impl2, impl3, impl4, impl5

RIGHT (vertical):
  RED→GREEN: test1→impl1
  RED→GREEN: test2→impl2
  RED→GREEN: test3→impl3
  ...
```

## Workflow

### 1. Planning

When exploring the codebase, read `CONTEXT.md` (if it exists) so that test names and interface vocabulary match the project's domain language, and respect ADRs in the area you're touching.

Before writing any code:

- [ ] Confirm with user what interface changes are needed
- [ ] Confirm with user which behaviors to test (prioritize)
- [ ] Identify opportunities for deep modules (small interface, deep implementation) — run the `/codebase-design` skill for the vocabulary and the testability checks
- [ ] List the behaviors to test (not implementation steps)
- [ ] Get user approval on the plan

Ask: "What should the public interface look like? Which behaviors are most important to test?"

**You can't test everything.** Confirm with the user exactly which behaviors matter most. Focus testing effort on critical paths and complex logic, not every possible edge case.

### 2. Tracer Bullet

Write ONE test that confirms ONE thing about the system:

```
RED:   Write test for first behavior → test fails
GREEN: Write minimal code to pass → test passes
```

This is your tracer bullet - proves the path works end-to-end.

### 3. Incremental Loop

For each remaining behavior:

```
RED:   Write next test → fails
GREEN: Minimal code to pass → passes
```

Rules:

- One test at a time
- Only enough code to pass current test
- Don't anticipate future tests
- Keep tests focused on observable behavior

### 4. Refactor

After all tests pass, look for [refactor candidates](refactoring.md):

- [ ] Extract duplication
- [ ] Deepen modules (move complexity behind simple interfaces)
- [ ] Apply SOLID principles where natural
- [ ] Consider what new code reveals about existing code
- [ ] Run tests after each refactor step

**Never refactor while RED.** Get to GREEN first.

## Checklist Per Cycle

```
[ ] Test describes behavior, not implementation
[ ] Test uses public interface only
[ ] Test would survive internal refactor
[ ] Code is minimal for this test
[ ] No speculative features added
```

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The session covered multiple features:
   - **Quick-access icon strips** in collapsed sidebars (left: source icons, right: studio tool + output icons)
   - **Subtle icon colors** for studio tools (violet/orange/emerald/sky/amber) with tinted `10%` opacity backgrounds
   - **E2E test fixes** for quiz tests: timeout reduction (10min→60s), `createMockQuizAndWait` navigation fix, `getByRole("menuitem")` to avoid strict-mode violations
   - **Issue #14 + #8 closure** on GitHub
   - **`/dev-agent` skill** creation — monitors log file for `[Task] Agent {name}: ` pattern, grills user, implements fix
   - **`devTodo` enhancement** — custom DOM modal with Bob/Alice/Eve buttons before POST request
   - **Karteikarten grilling session** — full requirements for Issue #20 gathered via grilling
   - **Hydration error fix** — `DropdownMenuTrigger asChild + <Button>` → direct className on trigger
   - **DB connection exhaustion fix** — globalThis singleton + `max: 5`
   - **Parallel upload progress fix** — `activeUpload` single → `activeUploads` array
   - **Issue #20 implementation start** — user asked to begin Karteikarten feature

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js App Router, hot-reload globalThis singleton pattern
   - Playwright E2E tests: `getByRole("menuitem")`, `aria-label`, route mocking
   - CSS 3D-Flip animation for flashcards
   - `postgres` connection pooling, Supabase connection limits
   - Base-ui `DropdownMenuTrigger` does NOT support `asChild` (unlike Radix)
   - Custom DOM modal (no React) for `devTodo` agent picker
   - Per-sourceId polling with cancellation controllers array
   - TDD vertical slice approach (tracer bullet first)
   - `generateObject` from Vercel AI SDK for structured LLM output
   - `studioOutputs` table with `type` + `data` JSON fields

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`components/notebook/source-sidebar.tsx`**
     - Added always-visible smart header (shows "Quellen" when expanded)
     - Removed duplicate header from expanded state
     - Added collapsed icon strip (+ button + source icons)
     - Changed `activeUpload: ActiveUpload | null` → `activeUploads: ActiveUpload[]`
     - Per-sourceId pollers with `activeUploadIds` join dependency
     - `handleMinimize` appends to array; `handleCancelUpload(sourceId)` removes by id
     - Render maps over `activeUploads`

   - **`components/notebook/studio-sidebar.tsx`**
     - Added `color` + `bg` to STUDIO_TOOLS: `{ color: "text-violet-400", bg: "bg-violet-500/10" }` etc.
     - `outputIcon()` uses colored classes per type
     - Added collapsed icon strip for tools + outputs
     - Added `aria-label="Zurück"` to quiz back button
     - Tool grid buttons: `cn(..., tool.bg)` + `hover:brightness-95 dark:hover:brightness-110`

   - **`lib/dev-todo.ts`** — custom DOM modal (`pickAgent()` Promise), `devTodo` now async:
     ```ts
     const AGENT_NAMES = ["Bob", "Alice", "Eve"] as const
     function pickAgent(): Promise<string | null> { /* DOM modal */ }
     export async function devTodo(feature: string) {
       if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "development") return
       const name = await pickAgent()
       if (!name) return
       fetch("/api/dev/todo", { method: "POST", body: JSON.stringify({ message: feature, name }) })
     }
     ```

   - **`app/api/dev/todo/route.ts`**:
     ```ts
     const { message, name } = (await req.json()) as { message: string; name: string }
     console.warn(`[Task] Agent ${name}: User clicked '${message}' — start a /grilling session before coding.`)
     ```

   - **`db/index.ts`** — singleton fix:
     ```ts
     const globalForDb = globalThis as unknown as { _pgClient?: postgres.Sql }
     const client = globalForDb._pgClient ?? postgres(process.env.DATABASE_URL!, { max: 5 })
     if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production") globalForDb._pgClient = client
     export const db = drizzle(client, { schema })
     ```

   - **`components/notebook/settings-dropdown.tsx`** — hydration fix:
     ```tsx
     <DropdownMenuTrigger className="inline-flex h-8 items-center gap-1.5 rounded-md px-3 text-xs font-medium transition-colors hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground">
       <Settings className="size-3.5" />
       {t("title")}
     </DropdownMenuTrigger>
     ```

   - **`playwright.test.config.ts`** — `timeout: 60 * 1000` (was 10min)

   - **`tests/quiz.local.spec.ts`**:
     - `createMockQuizAndWait`: waits for "Neu generieren", clicks "Zurück", finds `ul li`
     - All dropdown clicks: `getByRole("menuitem", { name: "..." })`
     - `tryDeleteNotebook`: `getByRole("menuitem", { name: "Löschen" })`

   - **`~/.claude/skills/dev-agent/SKILL.md`** — new skill: monitors log file, grills, fixes

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **E2E test timeout (10min)**: Reduced to 60s; root cause was `createMockQuizAndWait` searching for `li` that doesn't exist (quiz view opens automatically)
   - **"Zurück" button not found**: Added `aria-label="Zurück"` to quiz back button
   - **Strict mode violation `getByText("Umbenennen")`**: Notebook named "E2E Quiz Umbenennen" matched; fixed with `getByRole("menuitem", { name: "Umbenennen" })`
   - **Hydration error nested `<button>`**: `DropdownMenuTrigger asChild` + `<Button>` — base-ui doesn't support `asChild`; fixed by applying classes directly to trigger
   - **DB connection exhaustion**: `postgres()` called on every hot-reload; fixed with `globalThis` singleton + `max: 5`
   - **Single upload progress entry**: `activeUpload` single state replaced with `activeUploads` array with per-sourceId pollers
   - **Test Docker containers competing for DB connections**: `docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml down -v`

5. Problem Solving:
   - Collapsed sidebar icons reuse existing `SourceTypeIcon` / `outputIcon` functions — no new components needed
   - `devTodo` modal uses CSS variables (`var(--background)`, `var(--border)`) for automatic dark mode support
   - Parallel upload pollers use a `controllers` array pattern: each poller gets a `{ cancelled: boolean }` object; cleanup cancels all; pollers remove themselves from state when done
   - Poll effect depends on `activeUploads.map(u => u.sourceId).join(",")` — doesn't re-run on progress updates, only when sources are added/removed

## 🤖 Assistant

Sauber — 0 Errors. Der Fix: statt `[backface-visibility:hidden]` als Tailwind-Arbitrary-Value verwende ich jetzt `style={{ backfaceVisibility: "hidden" }}` und `style={{ transformStyle: "preserve-3d" }}` als inline Styles. Das ist bei CSS 3D-Transforms die zuverlässigste Methode, da Tailwind-Arbitrary-Values für `backface-visibility` in manchen Next.js-Setups nicht korrekt angewendet werden. Die Rückseite sollte jetzt kein Durchscheinen der Vorderseite mehr zeigen.

  - Der `←`-Button geht zur vorherigen Karte zurück

## 🧑 User

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   Implementing Issue #20 (Karteikarten/Flashcards) as a new Studio Tool, analogous to the Quiz feature. The full implementation includes: API route for generation, FlashcardView component with CSS 3D flip, result screen, "Nochmal üben" dropdown, StudioSidebar integration (tool button, view, dropdown menu, polling), i18n (de/en), share page support, and FlashcardCustomizeModal. The user then reported two visual bugs with the 3D flip (backface bleeding through), both now fixed. The most recent request (unanswered) is to redesign the rating button row into icon buttons with counts and reorganize the navigation layout.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js App Router, `after()` for background AI generation
   - Vercel AI SDK `generateObject` with Zod schema for flashcard generation
   - CSS 3D flip: `transformStyle: "preserve-3d"`, `backfaceVisibility: "hidden"`, `WebkitBackfaceVisibility: "hidden"`, inline styles required (Tailwind arbitrary values unreliable)
   - PostgreSQL enum extension via `drizzle-kit push`
   - `studioOutputs` table: type enum includes "flashcards"
   - Polling pattern: single interval, `pendingActionsRef`, `processedIdsRef`, `generatingIdsRef`
   - next-intl: ALL text in components must use `useTranslations` (no inline strings)
   - `useState<number>(() => Date.now())` for timer start (not `useRef(Date.now())` — lint `react-hooks/purity` error)
   - Queue-based card navigation: `queue: number[]` (indices into `data.cards`), `position` (index in queue)
   - Card ratings: `"known" | "unknown" | "skipped" | null` per card index

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`db/schema.ts`** — Added `"flashcards"` to `studioOutputTypeEnum`. Ran `npm run db:push` successfully.
     ```typescript
     export const studioOutputTypeEnum = pgEnum("studio_output_type", [
       "audio", "mindmap", "slidedeck", "datatable", "quiz", "flashcards",
     ])
     ```

   - **`app/api/studio/flashcards/route.ts`** — POST endpoint. Generates 20 cards (default) using `generateObject` with `FlashcardSchema = z.object({ cards: z.array(z.object({ front, back })).length(count), topics, suggestions })`. Uses `after()` for background generation, same pattern as quiz route.

   - **`app/api/studio/flashcards/[id]/route.ts`** — PATCH (rename) + DELETE, identical to quiz [id] route.

   - **`app/api/studio/flashcards/[id]/share/route.ts`** — POST creates/returns shareToken, identical to quiz share route.

   - **`components/notebook/flashcard-view.tsx`** — Main component. Key state:
     ```typescript
     const [queue, setQueue] = useState<number[]>(() => data.cards.map((_, i) => i))
     const [position, setPosition] = useState(0)
     const [flipped, setFlipped] = useState(false)
     const [ratings, setRatings] = useState<CardRating[]>(() => data.cards.map(() => null))
     const [mode, setMode] = useState<"study" | "results">("study")
     const [startTime, setStartTime] = useState<number>(() => Date.now())
     ```
     CSS 3D flip uses inline styles (critical for reliability):
     ```tsx
     <div style={{ perspective: "1200px" }}>
       <div style={{
         transformStyle: "preserve-3d",
         WebkitTransformStyle: "preserve-3d",
         transition: "transform 0.5s",
         transform: flipped ? "rotateY(180deg)" : "rotateY(0deg)",
       }} className="relative min-h-48 w-full">
         <div className="absolute inset-0 ... bg-card p-6"
              style={{ backfaceVisibility: "hidden", WebkitBackfaceVisibility: "hidden" }}>
           {/* Front */}
         </div>
         <div className="absolute inset-0 ... bg-card p-6"
              style={{ backfaceVisibility: "hidden", WebkitBackfaceVisibility: "hidden", transform: "rotateY(180deg)" }}>
           {/* Back */}
         </div>
       </div>
     </div>
     ```
     `restartSubset("all" | "unknown" | "skipped")` clears ratings for new queue and resets timer.
     Result screen has `CircleProgress`, stats (known/unknown/skipped/time), "Nochmal üben" dropdown (DropdownMenu), Behandelte Themen + Weiterlernen.
     Current action buttons (TO BE CHANGED per latest request):
     ```tsx
     {/* Actions */}
     {!flipped ? (
       <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
         <button onClick={skipCard}>Überspringen</button>
         <Button size="sm" onClick={() => setFlipped(true)}>Antwort ansehen</Button>
       </div>
     ) : (
       <div className="flex justify-center gap-3">
         <Button onClick={() => rate("unknown")}>Nicht verstanden</Button>
         <Button onClick={() => rate("known")}>Verstanden</Button>
       </div>
     )}
     {/* Navigation arrows */}
     <div className="mt-auto flex items-center justify-between border-t pt-3">
       <Button disabled={position === 0} onClick={() => navigate(-1)}><ChevronLeft /></Button>
       {/* dot indicators */}
       <Button disabled={position === queue.length - 1} onClick={() => navigate(1)}><ChevronRight /></Button>
     </div>
     ```

   - **`components/notebook/flashcard-customize-modal.tsx`** — Modal with sources view and customize view. Uses `useTranslations("flashcards")`. Count options: 10, 20 (standard), 50. Same structure as `QuizSourcesModal`.

   - **`messages/de.json`** — Added to `studio` section: `flashcards`, `flashcardsRegenerate`, `flashcardsGenerating`, `flashcardsNoSources`, `flashcardsError`. Added new `flashcards` section with all view/modal strings including `frontLabel`, `backLabel`, `showAnswer`, `known`, `unknown`, `skip`, `resultsTitle`, `time`, `skipped`, `practiceAgain`, `practiceAll`, `practiceUnknown`, `practiceSkipped`, `topicsCovered`, `learnMore`, `learnMoreHint`, `createNewDeck`, modal strings.

   - **`messages/en.json`** — Same structure as de.json with English translations.

   - **`components/notebook/studio-sidebar.tsx`** — Extensive changes:
     - Added `FaClone` from react-icons/fa, `FlashcardView`, `FlashcardData`, `FlashcardUsedSource`, `FlashcardCustomizeModal` imports
     - Added `{ id: "flashcards", labelKey: "flashcards", icon: FaClone, badge: null, color: "text-rose-400", bg: "bg-rose-500/10" }` to STUDIO_TOOLS
     - Added `case "flashcards": return <FaClone className="size-4 shrink-0 text-rose-400" />` to outputIcon
     - Added state: `activeFlashcards`, `flashcardsViewLoading`, `flashcardsModalOutput`
     - Added `activeFlashcardsRef` + useEffect to keep it current
     - `pendingActionsRef` type: `"open-quiz" | "create-slides" | "open-flashcards"`
     - Polling: handles `activeFlashcardsRef` update, `"open-flashcards"` action
     - `openFlashcards()`, `closeFlashcards()` functions
     - `generateFlashcards()` function: POST to `/api/studio/flashcards`
     - Header: `activeFlashcards ? tStudio("flashcards") : ...`
     - Collapse: `if (activeFlashcards) closeFlashcards()`
     - Tool button: `else if (tool.id === "flashcards") generateFlashcards()`
     - FlashcardView render section added between quiz and slidedeck views
     - `handleRenameStudioOutput`: `renamingOutput.type === "flashcards" ? "flashcards" : ...`
     - `handleDeleteStudioOutput`: same pattern + `if (activeFlashcards?.id === outputId) closeFlashcards()`
     - Flashcards dropdown: Share (→ `/api/studio/flashcards/${id}/share`), Umbenennen, Prompt-und-Quellen (→ `setFlashcardsModalOutput`), Löschen
     - `FlashcardCustomizeModal` added at bottom

   - **`app/share/[token]/page.tsx`** — Updated to handle both quiz and flashcards:
     ```tsx
     {output.type === "quiz" ? (
       <SharedQuizClient data={output.data as QuizData} outputId={output.id} />
     ) : output.type === "flashcards" ? (
       <SharedFlashcardsClient data={output.data as FlashcardData} outputId={output.id} />
     ) : notFound()}
     ```

   - **`app/share/[token]/shared-flashcards-client.tsx`** — New file, readOnly FlashcardView wrapper.

4. Errors and fixes:

   - **JSON syntax error in de.json (line 129):** `modalTopicPlaceholder` value contained `"` (curly quote) that was interpreted as closing the JSON string. Fixed by removing the inner quotes from the placeholder text.

   - **Lint error: `useRef(Date.now())` impure function call** (`react-hooks/purity`): Fixed by replacing `const startTimeRef = useRef(Date.now())` with `const [startTime, setStartTime] = useState<number>(() => Date.now())`, and removing `useRef` from imports.

   - **Bug 1 — Mirrored front text visible through back card:** Tailwind arbitrary values `[backface-visibility:hidden]` and `[transform-style:preserve-3d]` were not applying reliably. Fixed by switching to inline `style` props: `style={{ backfaceVisibility: "hidden" }}` on both faces and `style={{ transformStyle: "preserve-3d", transition: "transform 0.5s", transform: flipped ? "rotateY(180deg)" : "rotateY(0deg)" }}` on container.

   - **Bug 2 — Background still transparent (content bleeding through):** Two sub-issues: (a) Back card used `bg-primary/5` = 5% opacity = essentially transparent. Fixed by changing to `bg-card`. (b) Missing WebKit vendor prefixes. Fixed by adding `WebkitBackfaceVisibility: "hidden"` to both faces and `WebkitTransformStyle: "preserve-3d"` to container.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Used `queue: number[]` pattern for card ordering to support subset restarts ("nur falsch", "übersprungen") without needing to restructure data
   - Used `useState(() => Date.now())` (lazy initializer) instead of `useRef(Date.now())` to avoid lint error while still capturing start time
   - All 3D CSS uses inline styles rather than Tailwind arbitrary values for cross-browser reliability
   - Back card background is `bg-card` (same as front) to ensure full opacity — visual differentiation is achieved through label text ("RÜCKSEITE") not background color

6. All user messages:
   - "ja" — confirmed implementation plan
   - "machen wir weiter" — continue after dev server notification
   - (Screenshot showing mirrored text) "Irgendwas stimmt noch nicht. Auf der Rückseite kann man die Vorderseite spiegelverkehrt durchsehen, wie im screenshot." — first backface bug
   - (Screenshot still showing bleed-through) "die background color sollte nicht transparent sein. Ich kann immer noch durchgucken:" — second backface/transparency bug
   - "Bei den Karteikarten sollen die Buttons unten zum bewerten, ob man die frage korrekt beantwortet hat wie im screenshot als iconButtons dargestellt werden. Zudem soll das umdrehen per klick auf die Karteikarte funktionieren. Links neben dem 'flasch beantwortet'-Iconbutton soll ein pfeil nach links Iconbutton platziert werden, der zur vorherigen frage zurück geht und recht neben dem 'korrekt beantwortet'-Iconbutton ein pfeil nach rechts Iconbutton der die aktuelle frage überspringt." — UI redesign request for rating buttons

   **Security constraints (preserved verbatim):**
   - "NEVER print `.env` values — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`"
   - "NEVER edit `.env` files directly — only point out mismatches to the user"
   - "NEVER commit `.env` files"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Redesign rating buttons in FlashcardView** (latest unanswered request):
     - Rating buttons as icon buttons with count display (like screenshot: ✗ count | ✓ count)
     - Clicking the card flips it (not just the "Antwort ansehen" button)
     - Layout: `[← back-arrow]` `[✗ unknown-count]` `[✓ known-count]` `[→ skip-arrow]`
     - Left arrow = navigate(-1), ✗ = rate("unknown"), ✓ = rate("known"), right arrow = skip/navigate forward
   - Run `python3 scripts/export-chat.py` after feature completion (per memory: Chat-Export nach jedem Feature)

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before the summary request, the user sent a UI redesign request for the flashcard rating buttons with a screenshot reference (showing circular icon buttons with ✗ 1 and ✓ 0 counts). The request was: rating buttons should be icon buttons with counts, card click should flip, and the layout should be `[← prev]` `[✗]` `[✓]` `[→ skip]` as a single row.

   The current action/navigation section in `components/notebook/flashcard-view.tsx` (lines ~299-363) has two separate sections:
   - `{/* Actions */}` — conditional on `flipped` state: shows "Überspringen" + "Antwort ansehen" button (not flipped) OR ✗/✓ text buttons (flipped)
   - `{/* Navigation arrows */}` — always visible: ← dots → row

9. Optional Next Step:
   The user's most recent request (unanswered): "Bei den Karteikarten sollen die Buttons unten zum bewerten, ob man die frage korrekt beantwortet hat wie im screenshot als iconButtons dargestellt werden. Zudem soll das umdrehen per klick auf die Karteikarte funktionieren. Links neben dem 'flasch beantwortet'-Iconbutton soll ein pfeil nach links Iconbutton platziert werden, der zur vorherigen frage zurück geht und recht neben dem 'korrekt beantwortet'-Iconbutton ein pfeil nach rechts Iconbutton der die aktuelle frage überspringt."

   Next step: Edit `components/notebook/flashcard-view.tsx` to:
   1. Add `onClick={() => setFlipped(true)}` to the card container div (the `[perspective:1200px]` wrapper or the flip div itself)
   2. Replace the separate Actions + Navigation sections with a single bottom bar:
      - Always visible: `[← ChevronLeft]` `[X icon + unknown-count]` `[✓ icon + known-count]` `[→ ChevronRight]`
      - Before flip (or card not flipped): ✗ and ✓ are disabled, right arrow calls `skipCard()`
      - After flip: ✗ calls `rate("unknown")`, ✓ calls `rate("known")`, right arrow calls `skipCard()` or navigates
      - Left arrow always calls `navigate(-1)` (disabled at position 0)
      - Keep dot progress indicator (possibly move above or integrate into layout)
      - Add X (`XCircle` or similar) and Check (`CheckCircle2` or `Check`) lucide icons to the buttons
      - Show running counts: `stats.unknown` and `stats.known` (or session counts up to current position)

Zweite Frage: Die **„Formatvorschläge"**-Karten im Modal (Strategische Standortanalyse, Handbuch für Reisesicherheit etc.) sollen laut Issue kontextsensitiv von Claude generiert werden.

Das bedeutet: Modal öffnet sich → Lade-Spinner → Claude gibt 4 Vorschläge basierend auf den aktiven Quellen.

**Meine Empfehlung:** Für die erste Version die 4 Vorschläge **statisch hardcoden** (dieselben wie im Screenshot). Dynamische Vorschläge sind ein eigenes Feature mit eigenem Komplexitäts-Overhead (Extra-API-Call, Ladestate im Modal, Fehlerfall). Das kann Issue #22.1 werden.

## 🤖 Assistant

Gut. Dritte Frage: Die dynamischen Vorschläge brauchen einen eigenen API-Call. Wann wird er ausgelöst?

**Option A — Eager:** Sobald das Modal aufgeht → Skeleton-Karten für die Vorschläge, werden eingeblendet wenn Claude fertig ist.

**Option B — Lazy:** Erst wenn der User nach unten zum „Formatvorschläge"-Abschnitt scrollt.

**Meine Empfehlung: Option A (Eager).** Das Modal ist klein, der Call dauert 1–2 Sekunden, und der User sieht sofort dass da etwas lädt. Option B ist unnötige Komplexität für ein kleines Modal ohne echtes Scroll-Problem.

---

## 🧑 User

A

## 🤖 Assistant

Nächste Frage zur **Datenstruktur** des generierten Reports: Soll `section.content` **Markdown** oder **Plain Text** sein?

Markdown würde z. B. beim Lernplan Aufzählungen, Fettschrift und Quiz-Fragen als Liste ermöglichen. Plain Text ist einfacher zu rendern.

**Meine Empfehlung: Markdown.** Gerade Formate wie „Lernplan" (mit Glossar, Essay-Fragen) oder „Überblick" (mit Zitaten) profitieren davon. Rendering via `react-markdown` — das haben wir bereits im Stack?

**Meine Empfehlung: Inline in der Sidebar**, scrollbar — konsistent mit den anderen Tools. Einen Vollbild-Viewer bauen wir nur wenn der User es explizit braucht. Der Titel wird groß oben angezeigt, darunter die Sections mit `## Heading` + Markdown-Content.

## 🤖 Assistant

Alles grün. Implementierung vollständig:

- **`POST /api/studio/report`** — generiert strukturierten Markdown-Bericht mit `after()` + 202-Polling
- **`GET /api/studio/report/suggestions`** — lädt 4 kontextsensitive Formatvorschläge via Claude Haiku (schnell)
- **`ReportFormatModal`** — Zwei-Screen-Modal: Format-Auswahl (4 feste + dynamische Karten mit Stift-Icon) → Konfigurations-Ansicht (Sprache + Prompt)
- **`ReportView`** — inline in der Sidebar, Markdown-Sections mit `react-markdown`, Quellenliste am Ende
- **Studio Sidebar** — Tool-Button öffnet Modal, Polling-Action `open-report`, Detail-View mit Teilen/Umbenennen/Neu-generieren/Löschen-Dropdown
- **Share-Page** — Report-Typ direkt mit `ReportView` dargestellt

## 🧑 User

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Complete Issue #22: Implement a "Berichte" (Reports) Studio Tool for yannbooklm_v2, analogous to Quiz/Flashcards
   - UX flow: clicking the tool button opens a "Bericht erstellen" modal with format cards; clicking a card body generates immediately; clicking the pencil icon opens a config view (with the format card shown at top); clicking "Eigenen Bericht erstellen" opens config without a format card
   - Dynamic format suggestions loaded eagerly (skeleton while loading) from Claude via a separate GET endpoint
   - Report output: `{ title, sections: [{ heading, content }][] }` with Markdown content
   - Rendered inline in the sidebar (scrollable), same as Quiz/Flashcards

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js App Router with `after()` for background AI generation (202 polling pattern)
   - Vercel AI SDK `generateObject` with Zod schema for structured report generation
   - `generateObject` with `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` for cheap/fast suggestions endpoint
   - PostgreSQL enum extension via direct SQL (`ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS`) — drizzle-kit push has a bug with CHECK constraints
   - `studioOutputs` table: type enum extended with "report"
   - Polling pattern: single interval, `pendingActionsRef`, `processedIdsRef`, `generatingIdsRef`
   - next-intl: ALL text must use `useTranslations` (no inline strings), `messages/de.json` + `messages/en.json`
   - react-markdown already installed (`^10.1.0`), used for rendering section content
   - shadcn UI: `@/components/ui/select` and `@/components/ui/textarea` do NOT exist — must use native HTML `<select>` and `<textarea>` with Tailwind styling
   - Two-view modal pattern (formats view → config view) using `useState<"formats" | "config">`

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`db/schema.ts`** — Added `"report"` to `studioOutputTypeEnum`:
     ```typescript
     export const studioOutputTypeEnum = pgEnum("studio_output_type", [
       "audio", "mindmap", "slidedeck", "datatable", "quiz", "flashcards", "report",
     ])
     ```
     Applied via direct SQL: `ALTER TYPE studio_output_type ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'report'`

   - **`app/api/studio/report/route.ts`** — POST endpoint (new file):
     ```typescript
     const FIXED_FORMATS: Record<string, string> = {
       overview: "Erstelle einen strukturierten Überblick...",
       studyplan: "Erstelle einen Lernplan...",
       blogpost: "Schreibe einen aufschlussreichen Blogpost...",
     }
     // ReportSchema: { title: string, sections: [{ heading, content }][] }
     // after() pattern, same as quiz/flashcards
     ```

   - **`app/api/studio/report/suggestions/route.ts`** — GET endpoint (new file):
     ```typescript
     // Uses claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 for speed
     // Returns { suggestions: [{ title, description }][] } (4 items)
     // Falls back to { suggestions: [] } on error
     ```

   - **`app/api/studio/report/[id]/route.ts`** — PATCH + DELETE (new file, identical pattern to flashcards)

   - **`app/api/studio/report/[id]/share/route.ts`** — POST share token (new file, identical pattern to flashcards)

   - **`components/notebook/report-format-modal.tsx`** — New component:
     - Two views: `"formats"` (format selection) and `"config"` (language + prompt)
     - `FIXED_FORMATS = [{ id: "overview", titleKey, descKey }, { id: "studyplan"... }, { id: "blogpost"... }]`
     - `LANGUAGES = [{ value: "de", labelKey: "langDe" }, ...]`
     - Suggestions fetched via `fetch(/api/studio/report/suggestions?notebookId=...)` on modal open
     - `FormatCard` sub-component: card click = generate direct, pencil click = open config with card shown
     - Native `<select>` and `<textarea>` (NOT shadcn components — they don't exist)
     - Config view shows format card only when `config.showCard === true` (pencil path)

   - **`components/notebook/report-view.tsx`** — New component:
     ```typescript
     export type ReportSection = { heading: string; content: string }
     export type ReportUsedSource = { id: string; title: string; type: string }
     export type ReportData = {
       title: string
       sections: ReportSection[]
       format: string
       usedSources?: ReportUsedSource[]
     }
     export function ReportView({ data }: { data: ReportData }) { ... }
     // Uses react-markdown for section content rendering
     // Shows usedSources list at bottom
     ```

   - **`components/notebook/studio-sidebar.tsx`** — Many changes:
     - Added `FaNewspaper` from react-icons/fa import
     - Added `ReportView`, `ReportData`, `ReportFormatModal` imports
     - Added to STUDIO_TOOLS: `{ id: "report", labelKey: "report", icon: FaNewspaper, badge: null, color: "text-blue-400", bg: "bg-blue-500/10" }`
     - Added `case "report"` in `outputIcon()`
     - Added state: `activeReport`, `reportModalOpen`
     - Added `activeReportRef` + useEffect
     - Updated `pendingActionsRef` type to include `"open-report"`
     - Added polling: `if (activeReportRef.current?.id === output.id) setActiveReport(output)` and `"open-report"` action handler
     - Added `openReport()` / `closeReport()` functions
     - Updated `handleOutputClick` to handle `"report"` kind
     - Added `generateReport(format, language, customPrompt?, existingOutputId?)` function
     - Updated header title chain: `activeReport ? tStudio("report") : tStudio("title")`
     - Added `if (activeReport) closeReport()` in collapse handler
     - Added Report detail view render section (between mindmap and datatable in chain) with ← back + title + dropdown (share/rename/regenerate/delete)
     - Added `report` dropdown in output list (before the default quiz-else branch)
     - Updated tool button onClick: `else if (tool.id === "report") setReportModalOpen(true)`
     - Updated `handleRenameStudioOutput` apiBase to include report
     - Updated `handleDeleteStudioOutput`: added `if (activeReport?.id === outputId) closeReport()` + report in apiBase
     - Added updated rename to set activeReport, activeMindmap, activeDatatable
     - Added `<ReportFormatModal>` at bottom with `onGenerate={(format, language, customPrompt) => generateReport(format, language, customPrompt, activeReport?.id)}`

   - **`messages/de.json`** — Added to studio section:
     ```json
     "report": "Berichte",
     "reportRegenerate": "Neu generieren",
     "reportGenerating": "Bericht wird erstellt…",
     "reportNoSources": "Keine Quellen verfügbar. Bitte zuerst Quellen hinzufügen.",
     "reportError": "Fehler beim Erstellen des Berichts"
     ```
     Added new "report" section with: modalTitle, configTitle, back, formatCustomTitle/Desc, formatOverviewTitle/Desc, formatStudyplanTitle/Desc, formatBlogpostTitle/Desc, suggestionsTitle, suggestionsEmpty, languageLabel, langDe/En/Fr/Es/It, promptLabel, promptPlaceholder, generate, sourcesLabel

   - **`messages/en.json`** — Same structure with English translations

   - **`app/share/[token]/page.tsx`** — READ (not yet edited). Current content handles quiz, flashcards, mindmap. Report support needs to be added.

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **`drizzle-kit push` TypeError**: `checkValue.replace is not a function` — drizzle-kit bug with CHECK constraints during schema pull. Fixed by using direct SQL via `postgres` npm package: `ALTER TYPE studio_output_type ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'report'`
   - **Missing shadcn components**: `@/components/ui/select` and `@/components/ui/textarea` do not exist in this project. Fixed by replacing `<Select>`, `<SelectTrigger>`, etc. and `<Textarea>` with native HTML `<select>` and `<textarea>` styled with Tailwind classes:
     ```tsx
     <select className="border-input bg-background h-9 w-full rounded-md border px-3 text-sm shadow-sm focus:outline-none focus:ring-1 focus:ring-ring">
     <textarea className="border-input bg-background min-h-24 w-full resize-none rounded-md border px-3 py-2 text-sm shadow-sm focus:outline-none focus:ring-1 focus:ring-ring" />
     ```
   - **Incremental unused-variable warnings**: Expected during step-by-step wiring (FaNewspaper, ReportView, activeReport etc. flagged as unused before being referenced). All resolved as wiring progressed.
   - **File modified since read error**: On one Edit attempt for studio-sidebar.tsx, the linter had re-saved the file. Fixed by re-reading before editing.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Used direct SQL instead of drizzle-kit for enum extension
   - Used native HTML form elements instead of missing shadcn components
   - Followed established sidebar patterns (refs for polling closure, pendingActionsRef for post-generation navigation, same open/close function structure as all other tools)
   - ReportFormatModal design: "Eigenen Bericht erstellen" always shows config (blank), other format cards generate immediately on click but show config when pencil is clicked (with format card preview shown at top of config view)
   - Suggestions use claude-haiku (cheaper/faster) since they're UI-blocking on modal open

6. All User Messages:
   - "ja" — confirmed implementation plan for Issue #22
   - "deine Empfehlung passt" — confirmed: click card body = generate immediately, click pencil = open config
   - "A" — confirmed: eager loading for suggestions (load when modal opens, show skeletons)
   - "passt" — confirmed: Markdown content format
   - "Inline in der Sidebar, scrollbar" — confirmed: report view is inline in sidebar
   - "mache mit dem issue 22 weiter" — start implementing Issue #22
   - (Screenshot messages showing Berichte modal UI from Google NotebookLM)
   - "lege ein neues issue an..." — create GitHub issue #22 with full requirements
   - **Security constraints (verbatim):**
     - "NEVER print `.env` values — only check key existence: `grep -q "KEY" .env && echo set || echo missing`"
     - "NEVER edit `.env` files directly — only point out mismatches to the user"
     - "NEVER commit `.env` files"
   - "bitte mit --label aus dem issue" — use --label flag when running export-chat.py (session was renamed instead)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Add report support to `app/share/[token]/page.tsx`
   - Create `app/share/[token]/shared-report-client.tsx`
   - Run final typecheck
   - Commit all changes and close Issue #22
   - Run `python3 scripts/export-chat.py --label "Issue #22 Berichte"` (per memory: Chat-Export nach jedem Feature)

## 🤖 Assistant

Sauber. Hier eine Übersicht was gebaut wurde und was du noch einrichten musst:

---

**Implementiert:**

- **`types/google.d.ts`** — Konsolidierte globale Typen für `window.google` (picker + accounts) und `window.gapi`; `declare global` aus `google-slides-export.ts` und `google-docs-export.ts` entfernt
- **`lib/google-drive-picker.ts`** — Lädt GAPI + GIS, fragt OAuth-Token mit `drive.readonly` Scope an, öffnet den Google Drive Picker (gefiltert auf PDF + Google Docs), lädt die Datei client-seitig herunter (Google Docs werden als PDF exportiert)
- **`add-source-modal.tsx`** — Drive-Button aktiv mit Google Drive Logo, `"drive-downloading"` State mit Ladeanimation

**Du musst einrichten (einmalig in der Google Cloud Console):**

1. **Drive API aktivieren** — APIs & Services → Drive API → Enable
2. **OAuth Consent Screen** — `drive.readonly` Scope hinzufügen
3. **Optional:** `NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_API_KEY` in `.env` setzen (Google Picker API Key) — funktioniert in vielen Setups auch ohne, falls nicht kann ich es nachträglich draufschalten

---

## 🧑 User

<ide_selection>The user selected the lines 4 to 4 from Untitled-1:
tools deaktivieren wenn keine quelle vorhanden ist

This may or may not be related to the current task.</ide_selection>
Wenn ein Studio tool geklickt wird, obwohl noch keine quelle existiert oder keine quelle aktiv ist, soll der Name des Tools als Chatnachricht abgeschickt werden. Das LLM antwortet dann zb so:

"Das ist eine tolle Funktion! Mit einer Video-Zusammenfassung („Video Overview“) kannst du deine Inhalte als vertonte Präsentation mit KI-generierten Bildern aufbereiten. Du findest diese Option im Studio-Panel.
Damit das funktioniert, muss NotebookLM jedoch wissen, worum es geht. Da dein Notizbuch noch leer ist, ist der erste Schritt das Hinzufügen von Material im Quellen-Panel. Du kannst dort eigene Dateien (PDFs, Google Docs) hochladen oder Website-Links einfügen. NotebookLM nutzt diese Quellen dann als exakte Grundlage für dein Video, damit alles faktisch korrekt bleibt.
Hast du schon Dokumente, die du verwenden möchtest"

---

## 🤖 Assistant

Sauber. Was implementiert wurde:

- **`chat-panel.tsx`** — `notebook:ask` unterstützt jetzt `force: true`: Nachrichten werden auch ohne aktive Quellen gesendet (Source-Count-Check wird übersprungen)
- **`layout.tsx`** — berechnet `initialReadySourceCount` (status=ready + enabled=true) und gibt es weiter
- **`notebook-shell.tsx`** — leitet `initialReadySourceCount` als Prop durch
- **`studio-sidebar.tsx`** — trackt `activeSourceCount` (initial aus Props, Updates via `notebook:source-count-change`); wenn ein Tool-Button geklickt wird und `activeSourceCount === 0`, wird der Tool-Name als Chat-Nachricht mit `force: true` dispatcht statt zu generieren

Das LLM bekommt damit z.B. "Audio-Zusammenfassung" oder "Berichte" als Nachricht und kann kontextuell erklären was das Tool macht und auf das Hinzufügen von Quellen hinweisen.

---
